class="ArticleHeader_ah__N3ONy"><div class="ArticleHeader_ahBlock__sa_pW"><div class="ArticleHeader_ahCategory__UVU9k"><h3 class="ArticleHeader_ahCattext__YHYzU">Kia Rookie Ladder</h3><div class="DisplayAd_adTransitioner__G5t4A"><div class="GoogleDisplayAd_ad__Oum07" id="article_ad_s_1" data-ad-type="sponsor"></div></div></div><h1 class="ArticleHeader_ahTitle__qRVms">Kia Rookie Ladder: Durable rookie class keeps impressing</h1><p class="ArticleHeader_ahSubtitle__jGmAK">Cooper Flagg and Kon Knueppel are among the rookies showing resilience and availability all season long.</p></div><div class="ArticleHeader_ahBy__mKzod"><div class="ArticleHeader_ahAuthor__IteTb"><div class="ArticleAuthor_base__KkZ3k"><img src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2020/10/Aschburner.jpg" alt="Steve Aschburner" class="ArticleAuthor_authorPhoto__eRfLa"><div class="ArticleAuthor_bio__ClsuO"><div><p class="ArticleAuthor_authorName__oeNmP">Steve Aschburner</p></div><div class="ArticleAuthor_authorSocial__pENOl"><a href="/news/writers-archive/21/steve-aschburner" class="Anchor_anchor__wnmj2 Link_styled__TysPH" data-is-external="false" data-has-more="false" data-has-children="false">Archive</a></div></div></div><time class="ArticleHeader_ahDate__sR9IG">Updated on January 15, 2026 2:11 AM</time></div></div></div><div class="Columns_col__b1RWg"><div class="Columns_left__7mcek"><div class="ArticleContent_article__bAlgR"><div id="attachment_2003563" style="width: 1930px" class="wp-caption alignnone"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" aria-describedby="caption-attachment-2003563" class="wp-image-2003563 size-full" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel.jpg" alt="" width="684" height="384" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel.jpg 1920w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-784x441.jpg 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-1568x882.jpg 1568w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-768x432.jpg 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-1536x864.jpg 1536w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 1920px) 100vw, 1920px"><p id="caption-attachment-2003563" class="wp-caption-text">Cooper Flagg (left) and Kon Knueppel have each appeared in 39 of a possible 40 games so far this season.</p></div>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Not only have the Dallas Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg and the Charlotte Hornets’ Kon Knueppel excelled on the court to earn top spots here at the Kia Rookie Ladder, but they have mostly </span><i><span data-contrast="auto">stayed</span></i><span data-contrast="auto"> on the court, too. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Each has missed just one game so far, which is owed to their youth and spry physical shape. But it also speaks to their mental approach to surviving minor dings and pushing through without any string of absences. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Recently, the two former Duke teammates spoke separately about the grind and how they try to push through. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“I feel solid,” Flagg said when asked about his Mavericks workload. “It’s a lot of basketball, but I’m figuring out my routine — what works and what doesn’t. Some days you feel tired, some days you don’t feel 100 percent, but you play through it and figure it out.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Said Knueppel: “I think you just have to be willing to play. … Going in with that mindset: How can I have my body ready to play and perform every day?</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">“Like, not ‘Oh no, my ankle hurts, I’ve got to get some treatment today.’ It’s more like, ‘Alright, my shoulder tends to bug me, let’s get working on the shoulder throughout the week.’” </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Some form of injury accommodation is likely in both players’ future, but they’re not load-managing now, which gives maximum exposure to ticket-buying and TV-viewing fans around the league. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Here is this week’s Kia Rookie Ladder of the league’s top newcomers: </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>Weekly recap</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="auto"><strong>•</strong> <strong>Typically, this is a column</strong> where veteran players and coaches offer compliments and impressions of the new guys. But Philadelphia 76ers rookie VJ Edgecombe turned the tables recently when talking about teammate Kyle Lowry, whom he considers essential as a mentor. The young guard was delighted to see how much appreciation Lowry got when Philadelphia played at Toronto this week. “That was amazing,” he said. “The city shows that they still love him. … I was at home when they won the [2019] championship, so, like I said, for them to show love, credit to them. Credit to them for not being salty or anything.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto"><strong>•</strong> <strong>Special mention to Toronto’s Collin Murray-Boyles</strong>, who has been rolling in his past six games: 10.5 ppg, 8.2 rpg, 3.7 apg, two steals and one block, while shooting 60.9% and helping the Raptors go 4-2. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto"><strong>•</strong> <strong>Ryan Nembhard’s work off the bench for Dallas</strong> hadn’t been great (3.9 ppg, 21.4% 3-point shooting), but that’s where he’ll likely get his minutes once Kyrie Irving is back. So his play in reserve Monday in Brooklyn (nine points, eight assists, one turnover in 22 minutes) was a good sign. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<hr>
<h3>Storyline to watch <span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></h3>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">Beware the rookie wall. </span></b><span data-contrast="auto">This is the time of year rookies make like bugs drawn to one of those patio purple lights: </span><i><span data-contrast="auto">Zap</span></i><span data-contrast="auto">! The games have come fast and hard, rest has been sketchy and young fellas more used to playing 32 games in a whole season in college are pushing toward 40 or more in less than three months. One early player who seems to have gotten a rude introduction to the wall is San Antonio’s </span>Dylan Harper<span data-contrast="auto">, who went into Tuesday’s game at OKC averaging 2.3 points, three rebounds and 1.3 assists for the week, while shooting 16.7%. How Harper bounces back, and how many others in the Class of 2025 share a similar experience, will be something to watch. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<hr>
<p><i><span data-contrast="auto">(All stats through Tuesday, Jan. 13)</span></i><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>1. Cooper Flagg, Dallas Mavericks</h3>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> 19.1 ppg, 6.4 rpg, 4.3 apg<br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 1</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> ↔️<br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 1</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">It’s hard to find a flaw in Flagg’s game — frankly, his overall approach to this season as a teenage sensation — that he doesn’t address and fix. How ‘bout his work against Brooklyn Monday, when he notched his sixth game with at least 25 points, five rebounds and five assists – and this time didn’t turn over the ball in 35 minutes, while chipping in three steals and a block? </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<hr>
<h3>2. Kon Knueppel, Charlotte Hornets</h3>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> 19.1 ppg, 5.1 rpg, 3.6 apg </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 2 ↔️</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 4</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Knueppel looked a little vulnerable for slipping off this No. 2 rung on which he’s signed his name for much of the season, given his 30.4% shooting on 3-pointers and the end of his streak of games with multiple 3-pointers. But he did enough to hang on, and does enough regularly to impress another opponent. Here’s </span><a href="https://x.com/FlyByKnite/status/2010907908612243467"><span data-contrast="none">Clippers coach Ty Lue</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> on Monday talking about the Charlotte forward: “He plays at his own pace. He’s a 3-level scorer, he can get to the basket, mid-range, shoots the 3, and understanding how to play the game. He doesn’t force anything. … He was just too comfortable last game we played him.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<hr>
<h3>3. VJ Edgecombe, Philadelphia 76ers</h3>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> 16.1 ppg, 5.4 rpg, 4.4 apg </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 3</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> ↔️<br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 3</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Edgecombe couldn’t quite overtake Knueppel <a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026&DateFrom=01%2F07%2F2026">this week</a>, but the Sixers weren’t quibbling </span><a href="https://x.com/KyleNeubeck/status/2011081904393441510"><span data-contrast="none">with his production</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">, which included an average of 6.5 assists this week. He logged 38.2 minutes nightly and helped Philadelphia win three of four. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<hr>
<h3>4. Derik Queen, New Orleans Pelicans</h3>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> 12.8 ppg, 7.5 rpg, 4.3 apg</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 4 ↔️</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 13</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Praise from a kindred big-man spirit, Pelicans assistant coach Greg Monroe</span><a href="https://www.thebanner.com/sports/nba-derik-queen-rookie-pelicans-cooper-flagg-baltimore-GWGMNCDZL5FJXGJP7DCJ4U7A5M/"><span data-contrast="none">, reiterated what a lot</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> of rookie observers have noticed: this kid is <a href="https://www.nba.com/news/jeremiah-fears-derik-queen-pelicans-feature">completely unruffled</a> against the world’s best competition. Said Monroe: “He’s not intimidated. … There’s a moment when you think, ‘Wow, I’m actually playing against this dude.’ But he is not intimidated by anyone, and he is not lacking in confidence.” Queen, by the way, got his second triple-double of 14, 16 and 12 on his return to his roots at Washington on Friday — and he’s the only rookie who’s had even one. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<hr>
<h3>5. Cedric Coward, Memphis Grizzlies</h3>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> 13.9 ppg, 6.7 rpg, 2.9 apg </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 5</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> ↔️<br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 11</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Thanks in part to his 46.2% shooting on 3-pointers, Coward averaged 17 points, 8.5 boards and 2.5 assists <a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026&DateFrom=01%2F07%2F2026">this week</a>, while taking 6.5 of his 12 field-goal attempts from deep. He ranks second to Queen among the rookies in rebounding, and he scored in double figures 13 times in his past 14 games. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span><span data-contrast="auto">The next 5</span></h3>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">6. Maxime Raynaud, Sacramento Kings </span></b></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto"><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026">Season stats:</a> </span></b><span data-contrast="auto">10.5 ppg, 6.5 rpg, 1.1 apg</span><b><span data-contrast="auto"> </span></b><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder: </span></b></a>No. <span data-contrast="auto">7</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> ⬆️<br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick: </span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto">No. 42</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">When you’re a big man with skills and you’ve had the impact Raynaud has had (despite being drafted in the middle of the second round), teams — or at least some of their fans — might be second-guessing themselves. Minnesota is one such team, as it drafted a pair of project centers in June (Joan Beringer and Rocco Zikarsky), but could have had Raynaud for </span><a href="https://dunkingwithwolves.com/timberwolves-draft-regret-grows-as-ideal-center-of-the-future-maxime-raynaud-thrives-elsewhere"><span data-contrast="none">more current help</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">7. Egor Dëmin, Brooklyn Nets </span></b><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240,"469777462":[1110],"469777927":[0],"469777928":[1]}"> </span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto">: 10.4 ppg, 3.3 rpg, 3.5 apg</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240,"469777462":[1110],"469777927":[0],"469777928":[1]}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto">: No. 9</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240,"469777462":[1110],"469777927":[0],"469777928":[1]}"> ⬆️<br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto">: No. 8 </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">In the half dozen games right before Dëmin sat out vs. Dallas on Monday, he had drained 24 of 46 3-pointers, a 52.2% clip. Curiously, he shot 4-of-19 from </span><i><span data-contrast="auto">inside</span></i><span data-contrast="auto"> the arc in that stretch of games. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">8. Jeremiah Fears, New Orleans Pelicans </span></b></p>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> 14.4 ppg, 3.6 rpg, 3.1 apg</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 8</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> ↔️<br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 7</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Fears is back to consistently scoring 10+ per game after a spate of single-digit performances around the holidays. He has averaged 16 points in his past six appearances — the rookie point guard has yet to miss a game — and had his share of moments </span><a href="https://x.com/PelicansNBA/status/2010448651336704459"><span data-contrast="none">like this</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">9. Caleb Love, Portland Trail Blazers </span></b></p>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats: </span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto">10.7 ppg, 2.7 rpg, 2.3 apg</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder: </span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto">Not ranked</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> ⬆️<br>
</span><b><span data-contrast="auto"><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order">Draft pick:</a> </span></b><span data-contrast="auto">Undrafted</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">In early December, Love was hearing the word “inefficient” attached to his performances for Portland. But it’s hard to quibble with his production lately. <a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026&DateFrom=12%2F23%2F2025">Since Dec. 23</a>, he has averaged 16.8 points on 45% shooting, including 40.4% on 3-pointers. The word he’s starting to hear now: “</span><a href="https://x.com/realapp/status/2010522764579623404"><span data-contrast="none">Lillard</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">.” </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><b><span data-contrast="auto">10. Tre Johnson, Washington Wizards</span></b></p>
<p><a href="https://www.nba.com/stats/players/traditional?PlayerExperience=Rookie&DateTo=01%2F13%2F2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Season stats:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> 12.5 ppg, 3 rpg, 1.9 apg </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/kia-rookie-ladder-jan-7-2026"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Last Ladder:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 10 ↔️</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"><br>
</span><a href="https://www.nba.com/news/2025-nba-draft-order"><b><span data-contrast="auto">Draft pick:</span></b></a><span data-contrast="auto"> No. 6 </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p><span data-contrast="auto">Johnson continues to up his scoring for Washington, and earned praise from the Phoenix Suns’ Devin Booker after Johnson scored 19 in the Wizards’ road loss to the Suns on Sunday. “He’s smooth. He can shoot the ball. He can do a little bit of everything,” Booker said. Of hearing the rookie compared to him, Booker added: “I’ll take that after what he was doing tonight. He looks like a special talent.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559738":120,"335559739":280,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p>* * *</p>

Aschburner","firstName":"Steve","lastName":"Aschburner","organization":"","isNBAStaff":false,"authorPhoto":"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2020/10/Aschburner.jpg","socialHandle":"","socialLink":"","wpseoTitle":"","wpseoMetadesc":"","description":"","userUrl":"","featuredAuthor":true},"contentText":"Not only have the Dallas Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg and the Charlotte Hornets' Kon Knueppel excelled on the court to earn top spots here at the Kia Rookie Ladder, but they have mostly stayed on the court, too.  \r\n\r\nEach has missed just one game so far, which is owed to their youth and spry physical shape. But it also speaks to their mental approach to surviving minor dings and pushing through without any string of absences.  \r\n\r\nRecently, the two former Duke teammates spoke separately about the grind and how they try to push through.  \r\n\r\n“I feel solid,” Flagg said when asked about his Mavericks workload. “It’s a lot of basketball, but I’m figuring out my routine — what works and what doesn’t. Some days you feel tired, some days you don’t feel 100 percent, but you play through it and figure it out.” \r\n\r\nSaid Knueppel: “I think you just have to be willing to play. ... Going in with that mindset: How can I have my body ready to play and perform every day? \r\n\r\n“Like, not ‘Oh no, my ankle hurts, I've got to get some treatment today.’ It's more like, ‘Alright, my shoulder tends to bug me, let's get working on the shoulder throughout the week.’”  \r\n\r\nSome form of injury accommodation is likely in both players’ future, but they’re not load-managing now, which gives maximum exposure to ticket-buying and TV-viewing fans around the league.  \r\n\r\nHere is this week’s Kia Rookie Ladder of the league’s top newcomers:  \r\nWeekly recap\r\n• Typically, this is a column where veteran players and coaches offer compliments and impressions of the new guys. But Philadelphia 76ers rookie VJ Edgecombe turned the tables recently when talking about teammate Kyle Lowry, whom he considers essential as a mentor. The young guard was delighted to see how much appreciation Lowry got when Philadelphia played at Toronto this week. “That was amazing,” he said. “The city shows that they still love him. … I was at home when they won the [2019] championship, so, like I said, for them to show love, credit to them. Credit to them for not being salty or anything.” \r\n\r\n• Special mention to Toronto’s Collin Murray-Boyles, who has been rolling in his past six games: 10.5 ppg, 8.2 rpg, 3.7 apg, two steals and one block, while shooting 60.9% and helping the Raptors go 4-2.  \r\n\r\n• Ryan Nembhard’s work off the bench for Dallas hadn’t been great (3.9 ppg, 21.4% 3-point shooting), but that’s where he’ll likely get his minutes once Kyrie Irving is back. So his play in reserve Monday in Brooklyn (nine points, eight assists, one turnover in 22 minutes) was a good sign.  \r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nStoryline to watch  \r\nBeware the rookie wall. This is the time of year rookies make like bugs drawn to one of those patio purple lights: Zap! The games have come fast and hard, rest has been sketchy and young fellas more used to playing 32 games in a whole season in college are pushing toward 40 or more in less than three months. One early player who seems to have gotten a rude introduction to the wall is San Antonio’s Dylan Harper, who went into Tuesday’s game at OKC averaging 2.3 points, three rebounds and 1.3 assists for the week, while shooting 16.7%. How Harper bounces back, and how many others in the Class of 2025 share a similar experience, will be something to watch.  \r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n(All stats through Tuesday, Jan. 13) \r\n1. Cooper Flagg, Dallas Mavericks\r\nSeason stats: 19.1 ppg, 6.4 rpg, 4.3 apg\r\nLast Ladder: No. 1 ↔️\r\nDraft pick: No. 1 \r\n\r\nIt’s hard to find a flaw in Flagg’s game -- frankly, his overall approach to this season as a teenage sensation -- that he doesn’t address and fix. How ‘bout his work against Brooklyn Monday, when he notched his sixth game with at least 25 points, five rebounds and five assists – and this time didn’t turn over the ball in 35 minutes, while chipping in three steals and a block?  \r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n2. Kon Knueppel, Charlotte Hornets\r\nSeason stats: 19.1 ppg, 5.1 rpg, 3.6 apg \r\nLast Ladder: No. 2 ↔️\r\nDraft pick: No. 4 \r\n\r\nKnueppel looked a little vulnerable for slipping off this No. 2 rung on which he’s signed his name for much of the season, given his 30.4% shooting on 3-pointers and the end of his streak of games with multiple 3-pointers. But he did enough to hang on, and does enough regularly to impress another opponent. Here’s Clippers coach Ty Lue on Monday talking about the Charlotte forward: “He plays at his own pace. He’s a 3-level scorer, he can get to the basket, mid-range, shoots the 3, and understanding how to play the game. He doesn’t force anything. … He was just too comfortable last game we played him.” \r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n3. VJ Edgecombe, Philadelphia 76ers\r\nSeason stats: 16.1 ppg, 5.4 rpg, 4.4 apg \r\nLast Ladder: No. 3 ↔️\r\nDraft pick: No. 3 \r\n\r\nEdgecombe couldn’t quite overtake Knueppel this week, but the Sixers weren’t quibbling with his production, which included an average of 6.5 assists this week. He logged 38.2 minutes nightly and helped Philadelphia win three of four.  \r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n4. Derik Queen, New Orleans Pelicans\r\nSeason stats: 12.8 ppg, 7.5 rpg, 4.3 apg\r\nLast Ladder: No. 4 ↔️\r\nDraft pick: No. 13 \r\n\r\nPraise from a kindred big-man spirit, Pelicans assistant coach Greg Monroe, reiterated what a lot of rookie observers have noticed: this kid is completely unruffled against the world’s best competition. Said Monroe: “He’s not intimidated. … There’s a moment when you think, ‘Wow, I’m actually playing against this dude.’ But he is not intimidated by anyone, and he is not lacking in confidence.” Queen, by the way, got his second triple-double of 14, 16 and 12 on his return to his roots at Washington on Friday -- and he’s the only rookie who’s had even one.  \r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n5. Cedric Coward, Memphis Grizzlies\r\nSeason stats: 13.9 ppg, 6.7 rpg, 2.9 apg \r\nLast Ladder: No. 5 ↔️\r\nDraft pick: No. 11 \r\n\r\nThanks in part to his 46.2% shooting on 3-pointers, Coward averaged 17 points, 8.5 boards and 2.5 assists this week, while taking 6.5 of his 12 field-goal attempts from deep. He ranks second to Queen among the rookies in rebounding, and he scored in double figures 13 times in his past 14 games.  \r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n The next 5\r\n6. Maxime Raynaud, Sacramento Kings \r\n\r\nSeason stats: 10.5 ppg, 6.5 rpg, 1.1 apg \r\nLast Ladder: No. 7 ⬆️\r\nDraft pick: No. 42 \r\n\r\nWhen you’re a big man with skills and you’ve had the impact Raynaud has had (despite being drafted in the middle of the second round), teams -- or at least some of their fans -- might be second-guessing themselves. Minnesota is one such team, as it drafted a pair of project centers in June (Joan Beringer and Rocco Zikarsky), but could have had Raynaud for more current help.  \r\n\r\n7. Egor Dëmin, Brooklyn Nets  \r\n\r\nSeason stats: 10.4 ppg, 3.3 rpg, 3.5 apg\r\nLast Ladder: No. 9 ⬆️\r\nDraft pick: No. 8  \r\n\r\nIn the half dozen games right before Dëmin sat out vs. Dallas on Monday, he had drained 24 of 46 3-pointers, a 52.2% clip. Curiously, he shot 4-of-19 from inside the arc in that stretch of games.  \r\n\r\n8. Jeremiah Fears, New Orleans Pelicans \r\n\r\nSeason stats: 14.4 ppg, 3.6 rpg, 3.1 apg\r\nLast Ladder: No. 8 ↔️\r\nDraft pick: No. 7 \r\n\r\nFears is back to consistently scoring 10+ per game after a spate of single-digit performances around the holidays. He has averaged 16 points in his past six appearances -- the rookie point guard has yet to miss a game -- and had his share of moments like this.  \r\n\r\n9. Caleb Love, Portland Trail Blazers \r\n\r\nSeason stats: 10.7 ppg, 2.7 rpg, 2.3 apg\r\nLast Ladder: Not ranked ⬆️\r\nDraft pick: Undrafted \r\n\r\nIn early December, Love was hearing the word “inefficient” attached to his performances for Portland. But it’s hard to quibble with his production lately. Since Dec. 23, he has averaged 16.8 points on 45% shooting, including 40.4% on 3-pointers. The word he’s starting to hear now: “Lillard.”  \r\n\r\n10. Tre Johnson, Washington Wizards\r\n\r\nSeason stats: 12.5 ppg, 3 rpg, 1.9 apg \r\nLast Ladder: No. 10 ↔️\r\nDraft pick: No. 6  \r\n\r\nJohnson continues to up his scoring for Washington, and earned praise from the Phoenix Suns' Devin Booker after Johnson scored 19 in the Wizards’ road loss to the Suns on Sunday. “He's smooth. He can shoot the ball. He can do a little bit of everything,” Booker said. Of hearing the rookie compared to him, Booker added: “I'll take that after what he was doing tonight. He looks like a special talent.” \r\n\r\n* * *\r\n\r\nSteve Aschburner has written about the NBA since 1980. You can e-mail him here, find his archive here and follow him on X.","contentFiltered":"\u003cdiv id=\"attachment_2003563\" style=\"width: 1930px\" class=\"wp-caption alignnone\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2003563\" class=\"wp-image-2003563 size-full\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel.jpg\" alt=\"\" width=\"1920\" height=\"1080\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel.jpg 1920w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-784x441.jpg 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-1568x882.jpg 1568w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-768x432.jpg 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/01/flagg-knueppel-1536x864.jpg 1536w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 1920px) 100vw, 1920px\" /\u003e\u003cp id=\"caption-attachment-2003563\" class=\"wp-caption-text\"\u003eCooper Flagg (left) and Kon Knueppel have each appeared in 39 of a possible 40 games so far this season.\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan data-contrast=\"auto\"\u003eNot only have the Dallas Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg and the Charlotte Hornets\u0026#8217; Kon Knueppel excelled on the court to earn top spots here at the Kia Rookie Ladder, but they have mostly \u003c/span\u003e\u003ci\u003e\u003cspan data-contrast=\"auto\"\u003estayed\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/i\u003e\u003cspan data-contrast=\"auto\"\u003e on the court, too. \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan data-ccp-props=\"{\u0026quot;201341983\u0026quot;:0,\u0026quot;335559738\u0026quot;:120,\u0026quot;335559739\u0026quot;:280,\u0026quot;335559740\u0026quot;:240}\"\u003e \u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan data-contrast=\"auto\"\u003eEach has missed just one game so far, which is owed to their youth and spry physical shape. gold_text:
Kia Rookie Ladder: Durable rookie class keeps impressing
Cooper Flagg and Kon Knueppel are among the rookies showing resilience and availability all season long.

Not only have the Dallas Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg and the Charlotte Hornets’ Kon Knueppel excelled on the court to earn top spots here at the Kia Rookie Ladder, but they have mostly stayed on the court, too.  

Each has missed just one game so far, which is owed to their youth and spry physical shape. But it also speaks to their mental approach to surviving minor dings and pushing through without any string of absences.  

Recently, the two former Duke teammates spoke separately about the grind and how they try to push through.  

“I feel solid,” Flagg said when asked about his Mavericks workload. “It’s a lot of basketball, but I’m figuring out my routine — what works and what doesn’t. Some days you feel tired, some days you don’t feel 100 percent, but you play through it and figure it out.” 

Said Knueppel: “I think you just have to be willing to play. … Going in with that mindset: How can I have my body ready to play and perform every day? 

“Like, not ‘Oh no, my ankle hurts, I’ve got to get some treatment today.’ It’s more like, ‘Alright, my shoulder tends to bug me, let’s get working on the shoulder throughout the week.’”  

Some form of injury accommodation is likely in both players’ future, but they’re not load-managing now, which gives maximum exposure to ticket-buying and TV-viewing fans around the league.  

Here is this week’s Kia Rookie Ladder of the league’s top newcomers:  

Weekly recap
• Typically, this is a column where veteran players and coaches offer compliments and impressions of the new guys. But Philadelphia 76ers rookie VJ Edgecombe turned the tables recently when talking about teammate Kyle Lowry, whom he considers essential as a mentor. The young guard was delighted to see how much appreciation Lowry got when Philadelphia played at Toronto this week. “That was amazing,” he said. “The city shows that they still love him. … I was at home when they won the [2019] championship, so, like I said, for them to show love, credit to them. Credit to them for not being salty or anything.” 

• Special mention to Toronto’s Collin Murray-Boyles, who has been rolling in his past six games: 10.5 ppg, 8.2 rpg, 3.7 apg, two steals and one block, while shooting 60.9% and helping the Raptors go 4-2.  

• Ryan Nembhard’s work off the bench for Dallas hadn’t been great (3.9 ppg, 21.4% 3-point shooting), but that’s where he’ll likely get his minutes once Kyrie Irving is back. So his play in reserve Monday in Brooklyn (nine points, eight assists, one turnover in 22 minutes) was a good sign.  

Storyline to watch  
Beware the rookie wall. This is the time of year rookies make like bugs drawn to one of those patio purple lights: Zap! The games have come fast and hard, rest has been sketchy and young fellas more used to playing 32 games in a whole season in college are pushing toward 40 or more in less than three months. One early player who seems to have gotten a rude introduction to the wall is San Antonio’s Dylan Harper, who went into Tuesday’s game at OKC averaging 2.3 points, three rebounds and 1.3 assists for the week, while shooting 16.7%. How Harper bounces back, and how many others in the Class of 2025 share a similar experience, will be something to watch.  

(All stats through Tuesday, Jan. 13) 

1. Cooper Flagg, Dallas Mavericks
Season stats: 19.1 ppg, 6.4 rpg, 4.3 apg
Last Ladder: No. 1 ↔️
Draft pick: No. 1 

It’s hard to find a flaw in Flagg’s game — frankly, his overall approach to this season as a teenage sensation — that he doesn’t address and fix. How ‘bout his work against Brooklyn Monday, when he notched his sixth game with at least 25 points, five rebounds and five assists – and this time didn’t turn over the ball in 35 minutes, while chipping in three steals and a block?  

2. Kon Knueppel, Charlotte Hornets
Season stats: 19.1 ppg, 5.1 rpg, 3.6 apg 
Last Ladder: No. 2 ↔️
Draft pick: No. 4 

Knueppel looked a little vulnerable for slipping off this No. 2 rung on which he’s signed his name for much of the season, given his 30.4% shooting on 3-pointers and the end of his streak of games with multiple 3-pointers. But he did enough to hang on, and does enough regularly to impress another opponent. Here’s Clippers coach Ty Lue on Monday talking about the Charlotte forward: “He plays at his own pace. He’s a 3-level scorer, he can get to the basket, mid-range, shoots the 3, and understanding how to play the game. He doesn’t force anything. … He was just too comfortable last game we played him.” 

3. VJ Edgecombe, Philadelphia 76ers
Season stats: 16.1 ppg, 5.4 rpg, 4.4 apg 
Last Ladder: No. 3 ↔️
Draft pick: No. 3 

Edgecombe couldn’t quite overtake Knueppel this week, but the Sixers weren’t quibbling with his production, which included an average of 6.5 assists this week. He logged 38.2 minutes nightly and helped Philadelphia win three of four.  

4. Derik Queen, New Orleans Pelicans
Season stats: 12.8 ppg, 7.5 rpg, 4.3 apg
Last Ladder: No. 4 ↔️
Draft pick: No. 13 

Praise from a kindred big-man spirit, Pelicans assistant coach Greg Monroe, reiterated what a lot of rookie observers have noticed: this kid is completely unruffled against the world’s best competition. Said Monroe: “He’s not intimidated. … There’s a moment when you think, ‘Wow, I’m actually playing against this dude.’ But he is not intimidated by anyone, and he is not lacking in confidence.” Queen, by the way, got his second triple-double of 14, 16 and 12 on his return to his roots at Washington on Friday — and he’s the only rookie who’s had even one.  

5. Cedric Coward, Memphis Grizzlies
Season stats: 13.9 ppg, 6.7 rpg, 2.9 apg 
Last Ladder: No. 5 ↔️
Draft pick: No. 11 

Thanks in part to his 46.2% shooting on 3-pointers, Coward averaged 17 points, 8.5 boards and 2.5 assists this week, while taking 6.5 of his 12 field-goal attempts from deep. He ranks second to Queen among the rookies in rebounding, and he scored in double figures 13 times in his past 14 games.  

 The next 5
6. Maxime Raynaud, Sacramento Kings 

Season stats: 10.5 ppg, 6.5 rpg, 1.1 apg 
Last Ladder: No. 7 ⬆️
Draft pick: No. 42 

When you’re a big man with skills and you’ve had the impact Raynaud has had (despite being drafted in the middle of the second round), teams — or at least some of their fans — might be second-guessing themselves. Minnesota is one such team, as it drafted a pair of project centers in June (Joan Beringer and Rocco Zikarsky), but could have had Raynaud for more current help.  

7. Egor Dëmin, Brooklyn Nets  

Season stats: 10.4 ppg, 3.3 rpg, 3.5 apg
Last Ladder: No. 9 ⬆️
Draft pick: No. 8  

In the half dozen games right before Dëmin sat out vs. Dallas on Monday, he had drained 24 of 46 3-pointers, a 52.2% clip. Curiously, he shot 4-of-19 from inside the arc in that stretch of games.  

8. Jeremiah Fears, New Orleans Pelicans 

Season stats: 14.4 ppg, 3.6 rpg, 3.1 apg
Last Ladder: No. 8 ↔️
Draft pick: No. 7 

Fears is back to consistently scoring 10+ per game after a spate of single-digit performances around the holidays. He has averaged 16 points in his past six appearances — the rookie point guard has yet to miss a game — and had his share of moments like this.  

9. Caleb Love, Portland Trail Blazers 

Season stats: 10.7 ppg, 2.7 rpg, 2.3 apg
Last Ladder: Not ranked ⬆️
Draft pick: Undrafted 

In early December, Love was hearing the word “inefficient” attached to his performances for Portland. But it’s hard to quibble with his production lately. Since Dec. 23, he has averaged 16.8 points on 45% shooting, including 40.4% on 3-pointers. The word he’s starting to hear now: “Lillard.”  

10. Tre Johnson, Washington Wizards

Season stats: 12.5 ppg, 3 rpg, 1.9 apg 
Last Ladder: No. 10 ↔️
Draft pick: No. 6  

Johnson continues to up his scoring for Washington, and earned praise from the Phoenix Suns’ Devin Booker after Johnson scored 19 in the Wizards’ road loss to the Suns on Sunday. “He’s smooth. He can shoot the ball. He can do a little bit of everything,” Booker said. Of hearing the rookie compared to him, Booker added: “I’ll take that after what he was doing tonight. He looks like a special talent.” 

* * *

Steve Aschburner has written about the NBA since 1980. You can e-mail him here, find his archive here and follow him on X.

          </script><script id="tealium-script" type="text/javascript" async="" src="https://tags.nba.com/nba-web/prod/utag.js"></script><script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org/","@type":"Article","@id":"https://www.nba.com/news/nba-fantasy-top-10-performances-from-2025-26","headline":"NBA Fantasy: Top 10 performances from 2025-26","url":"https://www.nba.com/news/nba-fantasy-top-10-performances-from-2025-26","articleBody":"Play Yahoo Fantasy High Score Today!\r\n\r\nAs the fantasy basketball season winds down, let’s spotlight the top 10 single-game performances in Yahoo’s High Score format. Since only your best stat line each week counts, these games likely swung entire matchups. Here are the players who delivered the biggest stat lines through March 30 of the 2025-26 season.\r\n\r\nMy colleague Ben Zweiman has done an amazing job of keeping track of all the best performances each week, so I owe him a drink for making my life easier here.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n1. Bam Adebayo — 110 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nAdebayo scored 83!! points against the Wizards on March 10 — the second-highest single-game total in NBA history, behind only Wilt Chamberlain’s 100. He went 36-of-43 from the free-throw line (an NBA record), threw in a career-best 7 3s and the Heat ran a 57.5% usage rate through him all night. The 83 points alone drag this line to all-time territory. The 9 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 steals and 2 blocks just pile on.\r\n\r\nIt will likely go down as one of the most random, unexpected outlier performances in NBA history.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n2. Nikola Jokić — 108 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nThe Joker held the crown of the highest fantasy outing of the year until March 10 rolled around. His Christmas Day masterpiece against Minnesota was built on a gaudy 56-16-15 with 2 blocks. Of the players in the top 10 in fantasy scoring this season, this game featured the fewest stocks at 2. But that’s the thing about Jokić — the stocks aren’t as vital when you post modern-day Wilt Chamberlain numbers on a humble Christmas night. I’ve never rostered Jokić in all my years playing fantasy, but I would love to look at a box score, just one time and see numbers on the board like this.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n3. Cade Cunningham — 101 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nThe only player not named Adebayo or Jokić to crack 100 High Score points this season was Cunningham. Cunningham’s 46-12-11 triple-double back in November looked like a great fantasy night on its own, but it was his 7 stocks (5 steals and 2 blocks) that boosted his score another 21 points, helping him clear that 100-fantasy-point threshold. If he didn’t suffer a lung injury, he’d surely be in NBA MVP conversations and he was naturally one of the best fantasy contributors of the 2025-26 campaign.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n4. Luka Dončič — 99 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nOne point away from the 100-point club. Luka destroyed the Jazz on Dec. 18, posting a balanced 45 points, 11 rebounds, 14 assists and 5 steals. In the month of March, Dončič is making a strong case to earn his first MVP award, averaging 37-8-7 with 2 steals per game and leading the Lakers to a 14-2 record through Monday.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n5. Jalen Johnson — 98 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nJohnson came into the season too good to be a Most Improved Player; however, he still made a seismic leap into stardom. His ascension allowed the Hawks to pivot away from their franchise PG, Trae Young, because he was so impactful as a point-forward. Johnson is tied with Josh Giddey for the most triple-doubles this season (13), and his dominance on Nov. 13 factored into one of those 13. Johnson had 31-18-14 with an impressive 7 steals. It’s the two-way versatility and playmaking that morphed him into a first-round value this season.\r\n\r\nPlaying the Jazz also helps.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n6. Tyrese Maxey — 95 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nAnother player who's elevated to the fantasy elite is Maxey. He hung 54 points on the Bucks on Nov. 20, along with 9 assists, 5 rebounds and 6 stocks. Maxey’s defense has been a game-changer for his fantasy value, and seeing his peak at nearly 100 points was great, as he provided one of the highest yet consistent floors in High Score. He's been a top five asset across formats, averaging 54.8 fantasy points per night in high score.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n7. Nikola Jokić — 94 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nAgain, more Wilt numbers that have pretty much become normalized as just another day at the office for Nikola Jokić. The Warriors frontcourt posed no threat to Jokić on Feb. 22, where he amassed 35-20-12 with 5 stocks. Not quite his Christmas line, but still another dominant night from the best fantasy basketball player around.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n8. Kawhi Leonard — 94 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nThe only top-10 entry with fewer than 5 assists, but Kawhi put on a scoring and defensive clinic in late December, dropping an ethical and efficient 55 points with 11 boards, 2 assists and 8 stocks versus the Pistons. Putting up those numbers against the best team in the Eastern Conference says a lot about the type of season Leonard’s had. I thought he’d be great for High Score because you only had to rely on him for one game out of the week. However, he was barely load-managed and sits at 13th in fantasy points per game in High Score.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n9. Luka Dončič — 91 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nIt’s the second time we’ve seen Luka on this list, with this one coming in the midst of one of the best months of his career. Dončič fell one assist shy of the triple-double but delivered a masterpiece against the Bulls on March 12, scoring 51 points along with 10 rebounds, 9 assists, 3 steals and 1 block.\r\n\r\nTwo 90-point fantasy performances in the same season? Feels like there are only a few players capable of that each year and Luka’s one of ‘em.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n10. Nikola Jokić — 90 High Score points\r\n \r\n\r\n \r\n\r\nWhen you’re doing lists in fantasy basketball, the guy averaging a triple-double will be on it more than a couple of times. Jokić’s third and final entry in the top 10 is nearly a mirror of his Feb. 22 line: 31 points, 20 rebounds, 12 assists, 3 steals and 2 blocks in a comeback win at San Antonio. Given the tight Western Conference standings and the Spurs' post-All-Star break run, this was a significant statement game in both reality and class="wp-caption-text">The Pistons’ Cade Cunningham was the first player to eclipse 100 fantasy points in a game during the 2025-26 season.</p></div>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://basketball.fantasysports.yahoo.com/nba/signup/?ncid=100003119"><strong>Play Yahoo Fantasy High Score Today!</strong></a></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">As the fantasy basketball season winds down, let’s spotlight the top 10 single-game performances in Yahoo’s High Score format. Since only your best stat line each week counts, these games likely swung entire matchups. Here are the players who delivered the biggest stat lines through March 30 of the 2025-26 season.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">My colleague Ben Zweiman has done an amazing job of keeping track of all the best performances each week, so I owe him a drink for making my life easier here.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136630" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">1. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1628389/bam-adebayo">Bam Adebayo</a> — 110 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">Adebayo scored 83!! points against the Wizards on March 10 — the second-highest single-game total in NBA history, behind only <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/76375/wilt-chamberlain">Wilt Chamberlain</a>’s 100. He went 36-of-43 from the free-throw line (an NBA record), threw in a career-best 7 3s and the Heat ran a 57.5% usage rate through him all night. The 83 points alone drag this line to all-time territory. The 9 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 steals and 2 blocks just pile on.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">It will likely go down as one of the most random, unexpected outlier performances in NBA history.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136635" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">2. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/203999/nikola-jokic">Nikola Jokić</a> — 108 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">The Joker held the crown of the highest fantasy outing of the year until March 10 rolled around. His Christmas Day masterpiece against Minnesota was built on a gaudy 56-16-15 with 2 blocks. Of the players in the top 10 in fantasy scoring this season, this game featured the fewest stocks at 2. But that’s the thing about Jokić — the stocks aren’t as vital when you post modern-day Wilt Chamberlain numbers on a humble Christmas night. I’ve never rostered Jokić in all my years playing fantasy, but I would love to look at a box score, just one time and see numbers on the board like </span><i><span style="font-weight: 400">this</span></i><span style="font-weight: 400">.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136631" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">3. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1630595/cade-cunningham">Cade Cunningham</a> — 101 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">The only player not named Adebayo or Jokić to crack 100 High Score points this season was Cunningham. Cunningham’s 46-12-11 triple-double back in November looked like a great fantasy night on its own, but it was his 7 stocks (5 steals and 2 blocks) that boosted his score another 21 points, helping him clear that 100-fantasy-point threshold. If he didn’t suffer a lung injury, he’d surely be in NBA MVP conversations and he was naturally one of the best fantasy contributors of the 2025-26 campaign.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136632" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">4. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1629029/luka-doncic">Luka Dončič</a> — 99 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">One point away from the 100-point club. Luka destroyed the Jazz on Dec. 18, posting a balanced 45 points, 11 rebounds, 14 assists and 5 steals. In the month of March, Dončič is making a strong case to earn his first MVP award, averaging 37-8-7 with 2 steals per game and leading the Lakers to a 14-2 record through Monday.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136626" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">5. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1630552/jalen-johnson">Jalen Johnson</a> — 98 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">Johnson came into the season too good to be a Most Improved Player; however, he still made a seismic leap into stardom. His ascension allowed the Hawks to pivot away from their franchise PG, <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1629027/trae-young">Trae Young</a>, because he was so impactful as a point-forward. Johnson is tied with <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1630581/josh-giddey">Josh Giddey</a> for the most triple-doubles this season (13), and his dominance on Nov. 13 factored into one of those 13. Johnson had 31-18-14 with an impressive 7 steals. It’s the two-way versatility and playmaking that morphed him into a first-round value this season.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">Playing the Jazz also helps.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136629" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">6. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1630178/tyrese-maxey">Tyrese Maxey</a> — 95 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">Another player who’s elevated to the fantasy elite is Maxey. He hung 54 points on the Bucks on Nov. 20, along with 9 assists, 5 rebounds and 6 stocks. Maxey’s defense has been a game-changer for his fantasy value, and seeing his peak at nearly 100 points was great, as he provided one of the highest yet consistent floors in High Score. He’s been a top five asset across formats, averaging 54.8 fantasy points per night in high score.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136635" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">7. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/203999/nikola-jokic">Nikola Jokić</a> — 94 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">Again, more Wilt numbers that have pretty much become normalized as just another day at the office for Nikola Jokić. The Warriors frontcourt posed no threat to Jokić on Feb. 22, where he amassed 35-20-12 with 5 stocks. Not quite his Christmas line, but still another dominant night from the best fantasy basketball player around.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136627" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">8. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/202695/kawhi-leonard">Kawhi Leonard</a> — 94 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">The only top-10 entry with fewer than 5 assists, but Kawhi put on a scoring and defensive clinic in late December, dropping an ethical and efficient 55 points with 11 boards, 2 assists and 8 stocks versus the Pistons. Putting up those numbers against the best team in the Eastern Conference says a lot about the type of season Leonard’s had. I thought he’d be great for High Score because you only had to rely on him for one game out of the week. However, he was barely load-managed and sits at 13th in fantasy points per game in High Score.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136632" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">9. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/1629029/luka-doncic">Luka Dončič</a> — 91 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">It’s the second time we’ve seen Luka on this list, with this one coming in the midst of one of the best months of his career. Dončič fell one assist shy of the triple-double but delivered a masterpiece against the Bulls on March 12, scoring 51 points along with 10 rebounds, 9 assists, 3 steals and 1 block.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">Two 90-point fantasy performances in the same season? Feels like there are only a few players capable of that each year and Luka’s one of ‘em.</span></p>
<hr>
<h3><span style="font-weight: 400"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignleft wp-image-2136635" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png" alt="" width="100" height="73" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341.png 1040w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px">10. <a href="https://www.nba.com/player/203999/nikola-jokic">Nikola Jokić</a> — 90 High Score points</span></h3>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400">When you’re doing lists in fantasy basketball, the guy averaging a triple-double will be on it more than a couple of times. Jokić’s third and final entry in the top 10 is nearly a mirror of his Feb. 22 line: 31 points, 20 rebounds, 12 assists, 3 steals and 2 blocks in a comeback win at San Antonio. Given the tight Western Conference standings and the Spurs’ post-All-Star break run, this was a significant statement game in both reality and fantasy.</span></p>
