Yahoo.com","firstName":"","lastName":"","organization":"","isNBAStaff":false,"authorPhoto":"","socialHandle":"","socialLink":"","wpseoTitle":"","wpseoMetadesc":"","description":"","userUrl":"","featuredAuthor":false},"contentText":"Play Yahoo Fantasy High Score Today!\r\n\r\nAs the fantasy basketball season winds down, let’s spotlight the top 10 single-game performances in Yahoo’s High Score format. Since only your best stat line each week counts, these games likely swung entire matchups. Here are the players who delivered the biggest stat lines through March 30 of the 2025-26 season.\r\n\r\nMy colleague Ben Zweiman has done an amazing job of keeping track of all the best performances each week, so I owe him a drink for making my life easier here.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n1. Bam Adebayo — 110 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nAdebayo scored 83!! points against the Wizards on March 10 — the second-highest single-game total in NBA history, behind only Wilt Chamberlain’s 100. He went 36-of-43 from the free-throw line (an NBA record), threw in a career-best 7 3s and the Heat ran a 57.5% usage rate through him all night. The 83 points alone drag this line to all-time territory. The 9 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 steals and 2 blocks just pile on.\r\n\r\nIt will likely go down as one of the most random, unexpected outlier performances in NBA history.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n2. Nikola Jokić — 108 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nThe Joker held the crown of the highest fantasy outing of the year until March 10 rolled around. His Christmas Day masterpiece against Minnesota was built on a gaudy 56-16-15 with 2 blocks. Of the players in the top 10 in fantasy scoring this season, this game featured the fewest stocks at 2. But that’s the thing about Jokić — the stocks aren’t as vital when you post modern-day Wilt Chamberlain numbers on a humble Christmas night. I’ve never rostered Jokić in all my years playing fantasy, but I would love to look at a box score, just one time and see numbers on the board like this.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n3. Cade Cunningham — 101 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nThe only player not named Adebayo or Jokić to crack 100 High Score points this season was Cunningham. Cunningham’s 46-12-11 triple-double back in November looked like a great fantasy night on its own, but it was his 7 stocks (5 steals and 2 blocks) that boosted his score another 21 points, helping him clear that 100-fantasy-point threshold. If he didn’t suffer a lung injury, he’d surely be in NBA MVP conversations and he was naturally one of the best fantasy contributors of the 2025-26 campaign.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n4. Luka Dončič — 99 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nOne point away from the 100-point club. Luka destroyed the Jazz on Dec. 18, posting a balanced 45 points, 11 rebounds, 14 assists and 5 steals. In the month of March, Dončič is making a strong case to earn his first MVP award, averaging 37-8-7 with 2 steals per game and leading the Lakers to a 14-2 record through Monday.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n5. Jalen Johnson — 98 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nJohnson came into the season too good to be a Most Improved Player; however, he still made a seismic leap into stardom. His ascension allowed the Hawks to pivot away from their franchise PG, Trae Young, because he was so impactful as a point-forward. Johnson is tied with Josh Giddey for the most triple-doubles this season (13), and his dominance on Nov. 13 factored into one of those 13. Johnson had 31-18-14 with an impressive 7 steals. It’s the two-way versatility and playmaking that morphed him into a first-round value this season.\r\n\r\nPlaying the Jazz also helps.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n6. Tyrese Maxey — 95 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nAnother player who's elevated to the fantasy elite is Maxey. He hung 54 points on the Bucks on Nov. 20, along with 9 assists, 5 rebounds and 6 stocks. Maxey’s defense has been a game-changer for his fantasy value, and seeing his peak at nearly 100 points was great, as he provided one of the highest yet consistent floors in High Score. He's been a top five asset across formats, averaging 54.8 fantasy points per night in high score.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n7. Nikola Jokić — 94 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nAgain, more Wilt numbers that have pretty much become normalized as just another day at the office for Nikola Jokić. The Warriors frontcourt posed no threat to Jokić on Feb. 22, where he amassed 35-20-12 with 5 stocks. Not quite his Christmas line, but still another dominant night from the best fantasy basketball player around.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n8. Kawhi Leonard — 94 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nThe only top-10 entry with fewer than 5 assists, but Kawhi put on a scoring and defensive clinic in late December, dropping an ethical and efficient 55 points with 11 boards, 2 assists and 8 stocks versus the Pistons. Putting up those numbers against the best team in the Eastern Conference says a lot about the type of season Leonard’s had. I thought he’d be great for High Score because you only had to rely on him for one game out of the week. However, he was barely load-managed and sits at 13th in fantasy points per game in High Score.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n9. Luka Dončič — 91 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nIt’s the second time we’ve seen Luka on this list, with this one coming in the midst of one of the best months of his career. Dončič fell one assist shy of the triple-double but delivered a masterpiece against the Bulls on March 12, scoring 51 points along with 10 rebounds, 9 assists, 3 steals and 1 block.\r\n\r\nTwo 90-point fantasy performances in the same season? Feels like there are only a few players capable of that each year and Luka’s one of ‘em.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n10. Nikola Jokić — 90 High Score points\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\n\u0026nbsp;\r\n\r\nWhen you’re doing lists in fantasy basketball, the guy averaging a triple-double will be on it more than a couple of times. Jokić’s third and final entry in the top 10 is nearly a mirror of his Feb. 22 line: 31 points, 20 rebounds, 12 assists, 3 steals and 2 blocks in a comeback win at San Antonio. Given the tight Western Conference standings and the Spurs' post-All-Star break run, this was a significant statement game in both reality and fantasy.","contentFiltered":"\u003cdiv id=\"attachment_2136625\" style=\"width: 794px\" class=\"wp-caption alignnone\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2136625\" class=\"wp-image-2136625 size-medium\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/GettyImages-2265345161_cropped_processed_by_imagy-784x441.jpg\" alt=\"\" width=\"784\" height=\"441\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/GettyImages-2265345161_cropped_processed_by_imagy-784x441.jpg 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/GettyImages-2265345161_cropped_processed_by_imagy-1568x882.jpg 1568w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/GettyImages-2265345161_cropped_processed_by_imagy-768x432.jpg 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/GettyImages-2265345161_cropped_processed_by_imagy-1536x864.jpg 1536w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/GettyImages-2265345161_cropped_processed_by_imagy-2048x1152.jpg 2048w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 784px) 100vw, 784px\" /\u003e\u003cp id=\"caption-attachment-2136625\" class=\"wp-caption-text\"\u003eThe Pistons\u0026#8217; Cade Cunningham was the first player to eclipse 100 fantasy points in a game during the 2025-26 season.\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://basketball.fantasysports.yahoo.com/nba/signup/?ncid=100003119\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePlay Yahoo Fantasy High Score Today!\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eAs the fantasy basketball season winds down, let’s spotlight the top 10 single-game performances in Yahoo’s High Score format. Since only your best stat line each week counts, these games likely swung entire matchups. Here are the players who delivered the biggest stat lines through March 30 of the 2025-26 season.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eMy colleague Ben Zweiman has done an amazing job of keeping track of all the best performances each week, so I owe him a drink for making my life easier here.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136630\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141044.549.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e1. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1628389/bam-adebayo\"\u003eBam Adebayo\u003c/a\u003e — 110 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eAdebayo scored 83!! points against the Wizards on March 10 — the second-highest single-game total in NBA history, behind only \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/76375/wilt-chamberlain\"\u003eWilt Chamberlain\u003c/a\u003e’s 100. He went 36-of-43 from the free-throw line (an NBA record), threw in a career-best 7 3s and the Heat ran a 57.5% usage rate through him all night. The 83 points alone drag this line to all-time territory. The 9 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 steals and 2 blocks just pile on.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eIt will likely go down as one of the most random, unexpected outlier performances in NBA history.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136635\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e2. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/203999/nikola-jokic\"\u003eNikola Jokić\u003c/a\u003e — 108 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eThe Joker held the crown of the highest fantasy outing of the year until March 10 rolled around. His Christmas Day masterpiece against Minnesota was built on a gaudy 56-16-15 with 2 blocks. Of the players in the top 10 in fantasy scoring this season, this game featured the fewest stocks at 2. But that’s the thing about Jokić — the stocks aren’t as vital when you post modern-day Wilt Chamberlain numbers on a humble Christmas night. I’ve never rostered Jokić in all my years playing fantasy, but I would love to look at a box score, just one time and see numbers on the board like \u003c/span\u003e\u003ci\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003ethis\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/i\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136631\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141035.110.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e3. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1630595/cade-cunningham\"\u003eCade Cunningham\u003c/a\u003e — 101 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eThe only player not named Adebayo or Jokić to crack 100 High Score points this season was Cunningham. Cunningham’s 46-12-11 triple-double back in November looked like a great fantasy night on its own, but it was his 7 stocks (5 steals and 2 blocks) that boosted his score another 21 points, helping him clear that 100-fantasy-point threshold. If he didn’t suffer a lung injury, he’d surely be in NBA MVP conversations and he was naturally one of the best fantasy contributors of the 2025-26 campaign.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136632\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e4. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1629029/luka-doncic\"\u003eLuka Dončič\u003c/a\u003e — 99 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eOne point away from the 100-point club. Luka destroyed the Jazz on Dec. 18, posting a balanced 45 points, 11 rebounds, 14 assists and 5 steals. In the month of March, Dončič is making a strong case to earn his first MVP award, averaging 37-8-7 with 2 steals per game and leading the Lakers to a 14-2 record through Monday.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136626\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141155.679.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e5. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1630552/jalen-johnson\"\u003eJalen Johnson\u003c/a\u003e — 98 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eJohnson came into the season too good to be a Most Improved Player; however, he still made a seismic leap into stardom. His ascension allowed the Hawks to pivot away from their franchise PG, \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1629027/trae-young\"\u003eTrae Young\u003c/a\u003e, because he was so impactful as a point-forward. Johnson is tied with \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1630581/josh-giddey\"\u003eJosh Giddey\u003c/a\u003e for the most triple-doubles this season (13), and his dominance on Nov. 13 factored into one of those 13. Johnson had 31-18-14 with an impressive 7 steals. It’s the two-way versatility and playmaking that morphed him into a first-round value this season.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003ePlaying the Jazz also helps.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136629\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141050.541.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e6. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1630178/tyrese-maxey\"\u003eTyrese Maxey\u003c/a\u003e — 95 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eAnother player who\u0026#8217;s elevated to the fantasy elite is Maxey. He hung 54 points on the Bucks on Nov. 20, along with 9 assists, 5 rebounds and 6 stocks. Maxey’s defense has been a game-changer for his fantasy value, and seeing his peak at nearly 100 points was great, as he provided one of the highest yet consistent floors in High Score. He\u0026#8217;s been a top five asset across formats, averaging 54.8 fantasy points per night in high score.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136635\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T140957.341.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e7. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/203999/nikola-jokic\"\u003eNikola Jokić\u003c/a\u003e — 94 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eAgain, more Wilt numbers that have pretty much become normalized as just another day at the office for Nikola Jokić. The Warriors frontcourt posed no threat to Jokić on Feb. 22, where he amassed 35-20-12 with 5 stocks. Not quite his Christmas line, but still another dominant night from the best fantasy basketball player around.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136627\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141058.093.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e8. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/202695/kawhi-leonard\"\u003eKawhi Leonard\u003c/a\u003e — 94 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eThe only top-10 entry with fewer than 5 assists, but Kawhi put on a scoring and defensive clinic in late December, dropping an ethical and efficient 55 points with 11 boards, 2 assists and 8 stocks versus the Pistons. Putting up those numbers against the best team in the Eastern Conference says a lot about the type of season Leonard’s had. I thought he’d be great for High Score because you only had to rely on him for one game out of the week. However, he was barely load-managed and sits at 13th in fantasy points per game in High Score.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignleft wp-image-2136632\" src=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"100\" height=\"73\" srcset=\"https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-784x573.png 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813-768x561.png 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/03/image-2026-03-31T141019.813.png 1040w\" sizes=\"auto, (max-width: 100px) 100vw, 100px\" /\u003e9. \u003ca href=\"https://www.nba.com/player/1629029/luka-doncic\"\u003eLuka Dončič\u003c/a\u003e — 91 High Score points\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400\"\u003eIt’s the second time we’ve seen Luka on this list, with this one coming in the midst of one of the best months of his career. gold_text:
NBA Fantasy: Top 10 performances from 2025-26
Check out the best Yahoo Fantasy High Score performances from the 2025-26 NBA season.

Play Yahoo Fantasy High Score Today!
As the fantasy basketball season winds down, let’s spotlight the top 10 single-game performances in Yahoo’s High Score format. Since only your best stat line each week counts, these games likely swung entire matchups. Here are the players who delivered the biggest stat lines through March 30 of the 2025-26 season.

My colleague Ben Zweiman has done an amazing job of keeping track of all the best performances each week, so I owe him a drink for making my life easier here.

1. Bam Adebayo — 110 High Score points
Adebayo scored 83!! points against the Wizards on March 10 — the second-highest single-game total in NBA history, behind only Wilt Chamberlain’s 100. He went 36-of-43 from the free-throw line (an NBA record), threw in a career-best 7 3s and the Heat ran a 57.5% usage rate through him all night. The 83 points alone drag this line to all-time territory. The 9 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 steals and 2 blocks just pile on.

It will likely go down as one of the most random, unexpected outlier performances in NBA history.

2. Nikola Jokić — 108 High Score points
The Joker held the crown of the highest fantasy outing of the year until March 10 rolled around. His Christmas Day masterpiece against Minnesota was built on a gaudy 56-16-15 with 2 blocks. Of the players in the top 10 in fantasy scoring this season, this game featured the fewest stocks at 2. But that’s the thing about Jokić — the stocks aren’t as vital when you post modern-day Wilt Chamberlain numbers on a humble Christmas night. I’ve never rostered Jokić in all my years playing fantasy, but I would love to look at a box score, just one time and see numbers on the board like this.

3. Cade Cunningham — 101 High Score points
The only player not named Adebayo or Jokić to crack 100 High Score points this season was Cunningham. Cunningham’s 46-12-11 triple-double back in November looked like a great fantasy night on its own, but it was his 7 stocks (5 steals and 2 blocks) that boosted his score another 21 points, helping him clear that 100-fantasy-point threshold. If he didn’t suffer a lung injury, he’d surely be in NBA MVP conversations and he was naturally one of the best fantasy contributors of the 2025-26 campaign.

4. Luka Dončič — 99 High Score points
One point away from the 100-point club. Luka destroyed the Jazz on Dec. 18, posting a balanced 45 points, 11 rebounds, 14 assists and 5 steals. In the month of March, Dončič is making a strong case to earn his first MVP award, averaging 37-8-7 with 2 steals per game and leading the Lakers to a 14-2 record through Monday.

5. Jalen Johnson — 98 High Score points
Johnson came into the season too good to be a Most Improved Player; however, he still made a seismic leap into stardom. His ascension allowed the Hawks to pivot away from their franchise PG, Trae Young, because he was so impactful as a point-forward. Johnson is tied with Josh Giddey for the most triple-doubles this season (13), and his dominance on Nov. 13 factored into one of those 13. Johnson had 31-18-14 with an impressive 7 steals. It’s the two-way versatility and playmaking that morphed him into a first-round value this season.

Playing the Jazz also helps.

6. Tyrese Maxey — 95 High Score points
Another player who’s elevated to the fantasy elite is Maxey. He hung 54 points on the Bucks on Nov. 20, along with 9 assists, 5 rebounds and 6 stocks. Maxey’s defense has been a game-changer for his fantasy value, and seeing his peak at nearly 100 points was great, as he provided one of the highest yet consistent floors in High Score. He’s been a top five asset across formats, averaging 54.8 fantasy points per night in high score.

7. Nikola Jokić — 94 High Score points
Again, more Wilt numbers that have pretty much become normalized as just another day at the office for Nikola Jokić. The Warriors frontcourt posed no threat to Jokić on Feb. 22, where he amassed 35-20-12 with 5 stocks. Not quite his Christmas line, but still another dominant night from the best fantasy basketball player around.

8. Kawhi Leonard — 94 High Score points
The only top-10 entry with fewer than 5 assists, but Kawhi put on a scoring and defensive clinic in late December, dropping an ethical and efficient 55 points with 11 boards, 2 assists and 8 stocks versus the Pistons. Putting up those numbers against the best team in the Eastern Conference says a lot about the type of season Leonard’s had. I thought he’d be great for High Score because you only had to rely on him for one game out of the week. However, he was barely load-managed and sits at 13th in fantasy points per game in High Score.

9. Luka Dončič — 91 High Score points
It’s the second time we’ve seen Luka on this list, with this one coming in the midst of one of the best months of his career. Dončič fell one assist shy of the triple-double but delivered a masterpiece against the Bulls on March 12, scoring 51 points along with 10 rebounds, 9 assists, 3 steals and 1 block.

Two 90-point fantasy performances in the same season? Feels like there are only a few players capable of that each year and Luka’s one of ‘em.

10. Nikola Jokić — 90 High Score points
When you’re doing lists in fantasy basketball, the guy averaging a triple-double will be on it more than a couple of times. Jokić’s third and final entry in the top 10 is nearly a mirror of his Feb. 22 line: 31 points, 20 rebounds, 12 assists, 3 steals and 2 blocks in a comeback win at San Antonio. Given the tight Western Conference standings and the Spurs’ post-All-Star break run, this was a significant statement game in both reality and fantasy.

title: 10 storylines to follow during stretch run of regular season

  <esi:vars>
    <esi:choose>
      <esi:when test="$(HTTP_USER_AGENT) has_i 'iphone'">
        <esi:assign name="isMobile">true</esi:assign>
        <esi:assign name="device">ios</esi:assign>
      </esi:when>
      <esi:when test="$(HTTP_USER_AGENT) has_i 'ipad'">
        <esi:assign name="isMobile">true</esi:assign>
        <esi:assign name="device">ios</esi:assign>
      </esi:when>
      <esi:when test="$(HTTP_USER_AGENT) has_i 'android'">
        <esi:assign name="isMobile">true</esi:assign>
        <esi:assign name="device">android</esi:assign>
      </esi:when>
      <esi:otherwise>
        <esi:assign name="isMobile">false</esi:assign>
        <esi:assign name="device">computer</esi:assign>
      </esi:otherwise>
    </esi:choose>

    <esi:choose>
      <esi:when test="$(QUERY_STRING{'bypassCountry'})">
        <esi:assign name="bpc">$upper($url_encode($(QUERY_STRING{'bypassCountry'})))</esi:assign>
      </esi:when>
      <esi:otherwise>
        <esi:assign name="bpc">NONE</esi:assign>
      </esi:otherwise>
    </esi:choose>

    <esi:choose>
      <esi:when test="$(bpc)==''">
        <esi:assign name="country">$url_encode($(GEO{'country_code'}))</esi:assign>
      </esi:when>
      <esi:when test="$(bpc)=='NONE'">
        <esi:assign name="country">$url_encode($(GEO{'country_code'}))</esi:assign>
      </esi:when>
      <esi:otherwise>
        <esi:assign name="country">$(bpc)</esi:assign>
      </esi:otherwise>
    </esi:choose>

    <esi:choose>
      <esi:when test="$(country)=='US'">
        <esi:assign name="isDomestic">true</esi:assign>
      </esi:when>
      <esi:otherwise>
        <esi:assign name="isDomestic">false</esi:assign>
      </esi:otherwise>
    </esi:choose>

    <script>
      var _esi = {
        country: "$(country)",
        tz: "$(GEO{'timezone'})",
        region: "$(GEO{'region_code'})",
        browser: "$url_encode($(HTTP_USER_AGENT{'browser'}))",
        os: "$url_encode($(HTTP_USER_AGENT{'os'}))",
        device: "$(device)",
        isMobile: "$(isMobile)",
        isDomestic: "$(isDomestic)",
        ip: "$(REMOTE_ADDR)"
      };
    </script>
  </esi:vars>

          </script><script id="tealium-script" type="text/javascript" async="" src="https://tags.nba.com/nba-web/prod/utag.js"></script><script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org/","@type":"Article","@id":"https://www.nba.com/news/look-ahead-to-the-stretch-of-the-nba-season","headline":"10 storylines to follow during stretch run of regular season","url":"https://www.nba.com/news/look-ahead-to-the-stretch-of-the-nba-season","articleBody":"There’s often a tendency to label the post-All-Star break as the dog days of the regular season schedule, although this can also be misleading to a point. What if these dog days have bark? \r\nThe season isn’t over, and in some respects, a segment of the season is just beginning. There’s still so much up for grabs, so much unsettled, so much at stake. And it’ll require a month or maybe more to resolve. \r\nThe stretch run is where the championship contenders separate themselves from the pack and where the gap begins to expand between playoff hopefuls. Or maybe those gaps don’t expand; maybe they need an 82nd game. \r\nThese are the 10 pressing issues and developments waiting to be unraveled between now and mid-April — if not beyond:\r\n\r\n1. Jayson Tatum's Decision\r\nThis is by far the most substantial stretch-run must-see because of its obvious, far-reaching implications. If Tatum announces a return to the Boston Celtics— and even more, displays little to no rust — it could disrupt the power structure of the East and the league as well. \r\nThat’s how much Tatum, star of the 2024 championship run and All-NBA player out since injuring his Achilles last spring, means to the Celtics and how much a difference-maker of his magnitude can make in the playoffs. With a boost from Jaylen Brown, the Celtics are flourishing anyway without Tatum, currently at 35-19 and in second place. \r\nTatum has begun light workouts in a clear indication of an attempted return. If that happens, it must take place no later than March; Tatum would require multiple tune-ups to prepare for the intensity of the playoffs. \r\nBrown said Tatum is “checking off all the right boxes,” and Kevin Durant, who successfully returned from the same injury five years ago, has high hopes for Tatum. \r\n“I expect to see All-Star level play and the same Jayson Tatum we've seen before he was injured,” Durant said. “Usually around this time, you're playing a lot of five-on-five, playing against live bodies, just trying to get your rhythm and tempo back. So I'm looking forward to seeing him get out there with his team.” \r\n\r\n2. OKC at Full Strength\r\nThe 2025-26 season has been a unique one, to say the least, for the defending champion Thunder. They ripped through a 24-1 start and created comparisons with the 73-win Warriors. On the flip side, they’ve been spooked by the San Antonio Spurs, who have beaten OKC four times. \r\nThrough it all, OKC has never been fully healthy, with Jalen Williams missing 30 games, Isaiah Hartenstein 16 straight from December through January and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander the last five. \r\nThat’s about to change, and just in time. Williams is starting to regain his timing following off-season wrist surgery; Hartenstein is back from a calf strain and Shai’s abdominal injury was never projected to keep him from the lineup for a substantial stretch. \r\nWith a complete rotation that features the reigning MVP, solid defense and stretchy depth that added Jared McCain at the trade deadline, can the Thunder finish the season the way they started it and ride that momentum into the playoffs? \r\n\r\n3. Denver Looking for Dominance\r\nAn off-season of change helped reshape the Nuggets and position them for another attempted takeover of the West, helped largely by three-time MVP Nikola Jokić. That process remains on hold, though, because the Nuggets are still trying to find their footing and traction. \r\nJokić missed nearly a month after suffering a hyperextended knee, and the Nuggets’ rhythm has been off since his return; Denver is just 3-5 (although one loss was in overtime and two others were by a combined five points). While they’re getting positive play from Peyton Watson in an upgraded role, Aaron Gordon won’t be re-evaluated for another few weeks for his hamstring injury. \r\nSo, the Nuggets aren’t whole just yet. However, there’s upside. They’re on the clock, though, because the West is loaded and the margin for error just shrank. \r\n\r\n4. The Cleveland Connection\r\nDonovan Mitchell’s new backcourt running partner is James Harden and the honeymoon is blissful. The Cavs are undefeated in three games with Harden and are displaying a different dynamic. \r\nHarden is ninth on the all-time career scoring list yet seems more willing to rely on his passing skills, and perhaps justifiably so. Mitchell is a high-volume shooter and the other Cavs — Evan Mobley and Jarrett Allen in particular — require a creator. Harden is very good in that role; he worked wonders with Ivica Zubac with the Clippers, turning Zubac into a primary scoring option. Harden is averaging over nine assists with the Cavs. \r\nThis represents a major philosophical change by a contender in the middle of the season. Usually, these hasty transitional situations either don’t work or require more time. In this case, Harden might just be what Mitchell and the Cavs ordered. \r\n\r\n5. The Hornets' Hum\r\nIf there were an award for emerging out of nowhere, then the Charlotte Hornets would be a runaway winner. This team was dormant and stuck in the mud until right after the holidays. Suddenly the switch flipped and the Hornets took themselves out of the draft lottery conversation, and here they are, in the thick of play-in territory and rising. \r\nThe surprise is that the Hornets have done this without a major acquisition at the trade deadline. Their development is strictly the result of in-house growth and obviously the immediate impact of Kon Knueppel, a favorite for Rookie of the Year and already one of the league’s best shooters. \r\nLaMelo Ball is healthy and frisky. Charlotte has four players who can get 20 or more points a night — Ball, Knueppel, Brandon Miller, and Miles Bridges. The Hornets have won 10 of their last 11 with signature wins over the Spurs and Rockets. A seven-game home stand next month will tell; the Knicks, Sixers and Celtics (perhaps with Tatum) will pay a visit to an arena that’s suddenly swelling with noise. \r\n\r\n6. The Bucks Try to Bounce Back\r\nIt wasn’t too long ago when the Bucks seemed on the verge of collapse. The season started poorly, Myles Turner’s impact was marginal at best, they lost nine of 10 and then Giannis Antetokounmpo gave off weird vibes leading up to the trade deadline and while tending to a calf injury. \r\nGiannis wasn’t swapped at the deadline and the best the Bucks could do to improve the roster was sift through the bargain bin and add some players on the cheap. \r\nWell, Cam Thomas and Ousmane Dieng seem like perfect fits for a team that needed scoring and an emerging young talent. Meanwhile, Ryan Rollins and Kevin Porter Jr. are still making the most of their upgraded roles in the rotation. \r\nGiannis is due back any day from his injury. He remains a premier talent, and if he blends with his new surroundings, the Bucks, winners of five of their last six games without Giannis, could be a team no one wants to see in the playoffs. Provided Milwaukee gets there, of course. \r\n\r\n7. MVP Race\r\nThere's an interesting game on April 10, a final week tip-off that otherwise would seem relatively meaningless for two teams headed for the playoffs. Except: It’s OKC vs. Denver, meaning Shai vs. Jokić. Might the MVP race still be a toss-up at such a late stage? \r\nShai and Jokić are front-runners at the moment. Others are in the mix as well, but this race appears to be a carbon copy of last season’s when Shai emerged as the winner, mainly because OKC ran away with the best record in the West. There’s no such cushion this time between the teams. Therefore, suspense awaits. \r\n\r\n8. Rookie Race\r\nHas Cooper Flagg wrapped up the Rookie of the Year award? It appears so. There’s an argument that Flagg should’ve been the first rookie since Blake Griffin to make the All-Star Game (he didn’t, despite averaging 20 points and six rebounds). That’s how impactful he is for the Dallas Mavericks, and why the Mavs have already taken steps (trading Anthony Davis) to begin the process of building around their new centerpiece. \r\nIt will be Flagg or his Duke teammate, Knueppel, who’s averaging 19 points and shooting 43% from deep, taking honors. Actually, the only question is this: Why didn’t Duke win the national title with those two? \r\n\r\n9. Key Games in the Final Two Weeks\r\nSeveral teams will get pressed in the stretch run because they’ll either be boxing out for playoff seeding, trying to avoid the play-in tournament or trying to make the play-in cut.\r\nBecause of that, there’s almost guaranteed to be drama right before the close of the regular season. Here are four games to circle: \r\n\r\n\r\n \tPistons at Thunder, Mar. 30: Will these teams still hold a lead in their respective conferences? Being a top seed has its rewards. \r\n \tSpurs and Cavs at Warriors, April 1 and 2: This back-to-back could serve as the last stand for Stephen Curry, this season anyway, as the Warriors try to make a playoff push. They’re currently in eighth place and seemingly locked in the play-in tournament at the moment. \r\n \tRockets at Suns, April 7: Durant will see his old mates in a game that might drip with implications. Both Houston and Phoenix, along with others, are flirting with the sixth-place cutoff for guaranteed playoff spots; the Suns and Rockets are currently separated by two games in the West. \r\n \tCeltics at Knicks, April 9: These rivals are currently ranked second and third in the East. Remember, the second seed has home court advantage until the conference finals, and maybe even then, should the top seed tumble. \r\n\r\n10. LeBron and the Lakers\r\nIs the end approaching for a legend and a legendary franchise? LeBron James remains mum about his future plans; he can become a free agent this summer. Also, with a change in ownership, there has been no hint from the organization regarding where it stands on the subject. \r\nSo everything’s cloudy in LA. Also uncertain is the Lakers’ stretch run and how far it stretches. LeBron and Luka Dončić are All-Stars and the chemistry between the two seems fine. Austin Reaves is healthy again and impactful. But partly because of injuries, the Lakers haven’t given off contending vibes because defense and depth remain problematic. \r\n“When we've played some of our best basketball of the season, we've looked very good,” he said. “On the other side, when we've been terrible, we've looked disgusting.” \r\nWhenever the season ends in LA, only one question will beg for an answer: What next, LeBron? \r\n“I want to live,” he said over the weekend. “When I know, you guys will know. I don't know. I have no idea. I just want to live. That's all.” \r\n* * *\r\n\r\nShaun Powell has covered the NBA since 1985. data-has-nested-blocks="false" data-is-nested-block="false"><div class="Block_blockContent___l4IE Article_arBlock__km0zg"><article class="Article_arContainer__euQNQ"><div class="ArticleHeader_ah__N3ONy"><div class="ArticleHeader_ahBlock__sa_pW"><div class="ArticleHeader_ahCategory__UVU9k"><h3 class="ArticleHeader_ahCattext__YHYzU">Top Stories</h3><div class="DisplayAd_adTransitioner__G5t4A"><div class="GoogleDisplayAd_ad__Oum07" id="article_ad_s_1" data-ad-type="sponsor"></div></div></div><h1 class="ArticleHeader_ahTitle__qRVms">10 storylines to follow during stretch run of regular season</h1><p class="ArticleHeader_ahSubtitle__jGmAK">Here are 10 things that could happen, or not, in the final weeks of the season.</p></div><div class="ArticleHeader_ahBy__mKzod"><div class="ArticleHeader_ahAuthor__IteTb"><div class="ArticleAuthor_base__KkZ3k"><img src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2020/10/Powell.jpg" alt="Shaun Powell" class="ArticleAuthor_authorPhoto__eRfLa"><div class="ArticleAuthor_bio__ClsuO"><div><p class="ArticleAuthor_authorName__oeNmP">Shaun Powell</p></div><div class="ArticleAuthor_authorSocial__pENOl"><a href="/news/writers-archive/26/shaun-powell" class="Anchor_anchor__wnmj2 Link_styled__TysPH" data-is-external="false" data-has-more="false" data-has-children="false">Archive</a></div></div></div><time class="ArticleHeader_ahDate__sR9IG">Updated on February 18, 2026 1:39 PM</time></div></div></div><div class="Columns_col__b1RWg"><div class="Columns_left__7mcek"><div class="ArticleContent_article__bAlgR"><div id="attachment_2062406" style="width: 2058px" class="wp-caption alignnone"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" aria-describedby="caption-attachment-2062406" class="size-full wp-image-2062406" src="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/02/GettyImages-2259270267-scaled-e1771350061382.jpg" alt="" width="684" height="384" srcset="https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/02/GettyImages-2259270267-scaled-e1771350061382.jpg 2048w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/02/GettyImages-2259270267-scaled-e1771350061382-784x441.jpg 784w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/02/GettyImages-2259270267-scaled-e1771350061382-1568x882.jpg 1568w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/02/GettyImages-2259270267-scaled-e1771350061382-768x432.jpg 768w, https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2026/02/GettyImages-2259270267-scaled-e1771350061382-1536x864.jpg 1536w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 2048px) 100vw, 2048px"><p id="caption-attachment-2062406" class="wp-caption-text">Former Kia MVPs Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Nikola Jokić have their squads in the top half of the West standings.</p></div>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">There’s often a tendency to label the post-All-Star break as the dog days of the regular season schedule, although this can also be misleading to a point. What if these dog days have bark?</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">The season isn’t over, and in some respects, a segment of the season is just beginning. There’s still so much up for grabs, so much unsettled, so much at stake. And it’ll require a month or maybe more to resolve.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">The stretch run is where the championship contenders separate themselves from the pack and where the gap begins to expand between playoff hopefuls. Or maybe those gaps don’t expand; maybe they need an 82nd game.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">These are the 10 pressing issues and developments waiting to be unraveled between now and mid-April — if not beyond:</span></p>
<h3>1. Jayson Tatum’s Decision</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">This is by far the most substantial stretch-run must-see because of its obvious, far-reaching implications. If Tatum announces a return to the Boston Celtics— and even more, displays little to no rust — it could disrupt the power structure of the East and the league as well.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">That’s how much Tatum, star of the 2024 championship run and All-NBA player out since injuring his Achilles last spring, means to the Celtics and how much a difference-maker of his magnitude can make in the playoffs. With a boost from Jaylen Brown, the Celtics are flourishing anyway without Tatum, currently at 35-19 and in second place.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Tatum has begun light workouts in a clear indication of an attempted return. If that happens, it must take place no later than March; Tatum would require multiple tune-ups to prepare for the intensity of the playoffs.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Brown said Tatum is “checking off all the right boxes,” and Kevin Durant, who successfully returned from the same injury five years ago, has high hopes for Tatum.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">“I expect to see All-Star level play and the same Jayson Tatum we’ve seen before he was injured,” Durant said. “Usually around this time, you’re playing a lot of five-on-five, playing against live bodies, just trying to get your rhythm and tempo back. So I’m looking forward to seeing him get out there with his team.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>2. OKC at Full Strength</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">The 2025-26 season has been a unique one, to say the least, for the defending champion Thunder. They ripped through a 24-1 start and created comparisons with the 73-win Warriors. On the flip side, they’ve been spooked by the San Antonio Spurs, who have beaten OKC four times.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Through it all, OKC has never been fully healthy, with Jalen Williams missing 30 games, Isaiah Hartenstein 16 straight from December through January and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander the last five.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">That’s about to change, and just in time. Williams is starting to regain his timing following off-season wrist surgery; Hartenstein is back from a calf strain and Shai’s abdominal injury was never projected to keep him from the lineup for a substantial stretch.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">With a complete rotation that features the reigning MVP, solid defense and stretchy depth that added Jared McCain at the trade deadline, can the Thunder finish the season the way they started it and ride that momentum into the playoffs?</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>3. Denver Looking for Dominance</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">An off-season of change helped reshape the Nuggets and position them for another attempted takeover of the West, helped largely by three-time MVP Nikola Jokić. That process remains on hold, though, because the Nuggets are still trying to find their footing and traction.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Jokić missed nearly a month after suffering a hyperextended knee, and the Nuggets’ rhythm has been off since his return; Denver is just 3-5 (although one loss was in overtime and two others were by a combined five points). While they’re getting positive play from Peyton Watson in an upgraded role, Aaron Gordon won’t be re-evaluated for another few weeks for his hamstring injury.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">So, the Nuggets aren’t whole just yet. However, there’s upside. They’re on the clock, though, because the West is loaded and the margin for error just shrank.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>4. The Cleveland Connection</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">Donovan Mitchell’s new backcourt running partner is James Harden and the honeymoon is blissful. The Cavs are undefeated in three games with Harden and are displaying a different dynamic.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Harden is ninth on the all-time career scoring list yet seems more willing to rely on his passing skills, and perhaps justifiably so. Mitchell is a high-volume shooter and the other Cavs — Evan Mobley and Jarrett Allen in particular — require a creator. Harden is very good in that role; he worked wonders with Ivica Zubac with the Clippers, turning Zubac into a primary scoring option. Harden is averaging over nine assists with the Cavs.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">This represents a major philosophical change by a contender in the middle of the season. Usually, these hasty transitional situations either don’t work or require more time. In this case, Harden might just be what Mitchell and the Cavs ordered.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>5. The Hornets’ Hum</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">If there were an award for emerging out of nowhere, then the Charlotte Hornets would be a runaway winner. This team was dormant and stuck in the mud until right after the holidays. Suddenly the switch flipped and the Hornets took themselves out of the draft lottery conversation, and here they are, in the thick of play-in territory and rising.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">The surprise is that the Hornets have done this without a major acquisition at the trade deadline. Their development is strictly the result of in-house growth and obviously the immediate impact of Kon Knueppel, a favorite for Rookie of the Year and already one of the league’s best shooters.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">LaMelo Ball is healthy and frisky. Charlotte has four players who can get 20 or more points a night — Ball, Knueppel, Brandon Miller, and Miles Bridges. The Hornets have won 10 of their last 11 with signature wins over the Spurs and Rockets. A seven-game home stand next month will tell; the Knicks, Sixers and Celtics (perhaps with Tatum) will pay a visit to an arena that’s suddenly swelling with noise.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3 data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px">6. The Bucks Try to Bounce Back</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">It wasn’t too long ago when the Bucks seemed on the verge of collapse. The season started poorly, Myles Turner’s impact was marginal at best, they lost nine of 10 and then Giannis Antetokounmpo gave off weird vibes leading up to the trade deadline and while tending to a calf injury.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Giannis wasn’t swapped at the deadline and the best the Bucks could do to improve the roster was sift through the bargain bin and add some players on the cheap.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Well, Cam Thomas and Ousmane Dieng seem like perfect fits for a team that needed scoring and an emerging young talent. Meanwhile, Ryan Rollins and Kevin Porter Jr. are still making the most of their upgraded roles in the rotation.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Giannis is due back any day from his injury. He remains a premier talent, and if he blends with his new surroundings, the Bucks, winners of five of their last six games without Giannis, could be a team no one wants to see in the playoffs. Provided Milwaukee gets there, of course.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>7. MVP Race</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">There’s an interesting game on April 10, a final week tip-off that otherwise would seem relatively meaningless for two teams headed for the playoffs. Except: It’s OKC vs. Denver, meaning Shai vs. Jokić. Might the MVP race still be a toss-up at such a late stage?</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Shai and Jokić are front-runners at the moment. Others are in the mix as well, but this race appears to be a carbon copy of last season’s when Shai emerged as the winner, mainly because OKC ran away with the best record in the West. There’s no such cushion this time between the teams. Therefore, suspense awaits.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>8. Rookie Race</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">Has Cooper Flagg wrapped up the Rookie of the Year award? It appears so. There’s an argument that Flagg should’ve been the first rookie since Blake Griffin to make the All-Star Game (he didn’t, despite averaging 20 points and six rebounds). That’s how impactful he is for the Dallas Mavericks, and why the Mavs have already taken steps (trading Anthony Davis) to begin the process of building around their new centerpiece.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">It will be Flagg or his Duke teammate, Knueppel, who’s averaging 19 points and shooting 43% from deep, taking honors. Actually, the only question is this: Why didn’t Duke win the national title with those two?</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<h3>9. Key Games in the Final Two Weeks</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">Several teams will get pressed in the stretch run because they’ll either be boxing out for playoff seeding, trying to avoid the play-in tournament or trying to make the play-in cut.</span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Because of that, there’s almost guaranteed to be drama right before the close of the regular season. Here are four games to circle:</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<ul>
<li data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none"><strong>Pistons at Thunder, Mar. 30:</strong> Will these teams still hold a lead in their respective conferences? Being a top seed has its rewards.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none"><strong>Spurs and Cavs at Warriors, April 1 and 2:</strong> This back-to-back could serve as the last stand for Stephen Curry, this season anyway, as the Warriors try to make a playoff push. They’re currently in eighth place and seemingly locked in the play-in tournament at the moment.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none"><strong>Rockets at Suns, April 7:</strong> Durant will see his old mates in a game that might drip with implications. Both Houston and Phoenix, along with others, are flirting with the sixth-place cutoff for guaranteed playoff spots; the Suns and Rockets are currently separated by two games in the West.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none"><strong>Celtics at Knicks, April 9:</strong> These rivals are currently ranked second and third in the East. Remember, the second seed has home court advantage until the conference finals, and maybe even then, should the top seed tumble.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<h3>10. LeBron and the Lakers</h3>
<p><span data-contrast="none">Is the end approaching for a legend and a legendary franchise? LeBron James remains mum about his future plans; he can become a free agent this summer. Also, with a change in ownership, there has been no hint from the organization regarding where it stands on the subject.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">So everything’s cloudy in LA. Also uncertain is the Lakers’ stretch run and how far it stretches. LeBron and Luka Dončić are All-Stars and the chemistry between the two seems fine. Austin Reaves is healthy again and impactful. But partly because of injuries, the Lakers haven’t given off contending vibes because defense and depth remain problematic.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">“When we’ve played some of our best basketball of the season, we’ve looked very good,” he said. “On the other side, when we’ve been terrible, we’ve looked disgusting.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">Whenever the season ends in LA, only one question will beg for an answer: What next, LeBron?</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p data-ccp-border-bottom="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-bottom="0px" data-ccp-border-between="0px none #000000" data-ccp-padding-between="0px"><span data-contrast="none">“I want to live,” he said over the weekend. “When I know, you guys will know. I don’t know. I have no idea. I just want to live. That’s all.”</span><span data-ccp-props="{"134245417":true,"134245418":false,"134245529":false,"201341983":0,"335551550":1,"335551620":1,"335557856":4278190080,"335559685":0,"335559731":0,"335559737":0,"335559738":0,"335559739":0,"335559740":240}"> </span></p>
<p>* * *</p>
