class="ArticleHeader_ahCategory__UVU9k"><h3 class="ArticleHeader_ahCattext__YHYzU">Starting 5 Daily Newsletter</h3><div class="DisplayAd_adTransitioner__G5t4A"><div class="GoogleDisplayAd_ad__Oum07" id="article_ad_s_1" data-ad-type="sponsor"></div></div></div><h1 class="ArticleHeader_ahTitle__qRVms">Starting 5: Play-In thrills continue as Warriors, 76ers get wins</h1><p class="ArticleHeader_ahSubtitle__jGmAK">Stephen Curry erupts to stun Clippers, Tyrese Maxey and Sixers punch Playoff ticket and Friday’s Play-In finale is set.</p></div><div class="ArticleHeader_ahBy__mKzod"><div class="ArticleHeader_ahAuthor__IteTb"><div class="ArticleAuthor_base__KkZ3k"><div class="ArticleAuthor_bio__ClsuO"><div><p class="ArticleAuthor_authorName__oeNmP">NBA.com Staff</p></div><div class="ArticleAuthor_authorSocial__pENOl"></div></div></div><time class="ArticleHeader_ahDate__sR9IG">Updated on April 16, 2026 3:01 PM</time></div></div></div><div class="Columns_col__b1RWg"><div class="Columns_left__7mcek"><div class="ArticleContent_article__bAlgR"><div class="wp-caption"><div class="embed-wrapper"><iframe class="mkv-embed mkv-embed-2163722 mkv-embed-prod" data-nba-id="2163722" data-nba-env="prod" src="https://www.nba.com/watch/embed/2163722?autostart=false&mute=false" allowfullscreen=""></iframe></div><p class="wp-caption-text">76ers punch playoff ticket by grabbing 7th seed, Curry's 35 fuels Warriors comeback over Clippers in SoFi NBA Play-In Tournament action.</p></div>
<div style="width: 100%; max-width: 640px;">
<p><strong>A golden comeback</strong><strong>, </strong>capped by an instant-classic Steph Curry triple, in another Play-In thriller.<br>
<strong><br>
As coach Steve Kerr put it:</strong> ‘<a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://x.com/warriors/status/2044647192619888980?s=20&lid=4kbl5hxwcl9b" target="_blank" rel="noopener" data-mce-attribute-name-1="" data-mce-attribute-value-1="" data-mce-attributes-count="1">This is why Steph came back.</a>‘</p>
<p><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b1191ae69100641e27da/original.gif?1776333080" alt="Stephen Curry" width="264" height="264"></p>
<hr>
<h3><span class="tinyMce-placeholder">5 STORIES IN TODAY’S EDITION 🏀</span></h3>
<p><strong>Dubs Stun Clips:</strong> Curry’s vintage flurry, Horford’s epic 4th and an unreal clutch rally</p>
<p><strong>Warriors’ Way:</strong> With their season on the line, Golden State’s championship vets delivered</p>
<p><strong>Playoff Philly:</strong> Key contributions and Maxey’s 31 points move Sixers past Magic</p>
<p><strong>Friday Look-Ahead:</strong> Magic-Hornets, Warriors-Suns, winner to Playoffs, loser goes home</p>
<p><strong>Awaiting Friday’s Winners:</strong> Shaun Powell examines what’s ahead for both No. 1 seeds</p>
<hr>
<h3><span class="tinyMce-placeholder">BUT FIRST … ⏰</span></h3>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b168aed2580064d1d9f7/original.jpg?1776333159" alt="Scores & Schedule" width="720" height="450"></p>
<p><strong>The </strong><a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://www.nba.com/news/nba-play-in-tournament?lid=h145xryg2hdy"><strong>SoFi NBA Play-In Tournament</strong></a> sends its final two teams to the Playoffs – and two more home – on Friday, when the Magic host the Hornets (<a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://www.nba.com/game/0052500201?lid=kqx27qfma00f">7:30 ET, Prime</a>), followed by the Warriors visiting the Suns (<a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://www.nba.com/game/0052500211?lid=iq77ngoi8q56">10 ET, Prime</a>).</p>
<p><strong>Playoffs Saturday: </strong>Friday’s winners earn their conference’s No. 8 seed (but won’t play until Sunday). The <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://www.nba.com/playoffs/2026?lid=n825pd4efzjz">NBA Playoffs</a> tip off Saturday with four Game 1s across Prime and ABC, followed by four more Sunday on ABC and NBC.</p>
<p><strong>Viewership Jumps: </strong>170 million people in the U.S. watched NBA games across ABC/ESPN, Amazon Prime Video, NBC/Peacock and NBA TV during the regular season – <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://x.com/NBA/status/2044447933529989544?lid=yfd32u2a6gds">the most in 24 years</a> and up 86% from last season.</p>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b18617cd200064855548/original.jpg?1776333190" alt="Playoff Picture" width="720" height="405"></p>
<hr>
<h3>1. CURRY IGNITES WARRIORS PAST CLIPS IN ANOTHER PLAY-IN CLASSIC</h3>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b9f77eac8200644b5105/original.jpg?1776335350" alt="Stephen Curry" width="576" height="720"></p>
<p><strong>They had lost seven</strong> of their last eight games entering Wednesday.</p>
<p><strong>They were 8-33</strong> this year when trailing entering the 4th quarter.</p>
<p><strong>But with their season</strong> on the brink, staring at a 13-point deficit with 9:53 left in last night’s win-or-go-home showdown, the Warriors found a way.</p>
<p><strong>And in the biggest moment,</strong> it was Steph Curry.</p>
<p><strong>Warriors 126, Clippers 121:</strong> Curry (7 3s) dropped 27 of his <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://x.com/NBA/status/2044645883568890095?lid=gcd2j1vl14yw">35 points</a> in the 2nd half, willing Golden State back in the 4th alongside Al Horford (14 pts, 3 3s) as the Warriors closed on a 16-5 run, highlighted by Steph’s off-balance, go-ahead 3 with 50 seconds left to stun the Clippers. | <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://www.nba.com/game/gsw-vs-lac-0052500131?lid=fyoh79l4mvhl">Recap</a></p>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b28df1ba0e0065cb14d8/original.jpg?1776333452" alt="Al Horford, Stephen Curry" width="660" height="480"></p>
<p><strong>Steph Spark:</strong> After going down 10 early in the 2nd half, Curry caught fire for 16 points in the 3rd, drilling three triples in a three-minute span to cut the deficit to six entering the 4th.</p>
<p><strong>It was only</strong> the start.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Clips Clap Back:</strong> After holding multiple double-digit leads, Bennedict Mathurin (23 pts) and Darius Garland (21 pts, 8 ast) pushed LA’s edge back to 13 with 9:53</li>
<li><strong>Steph Says Hello:</strong> Moments later, Curry checked back in and immediately splashed a 3, jumpstarting a push that trimmed the deficit to seven</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Then, Al’s Eruption: </strong>With the comeback brewing, Horford brought the jolt: a 3 to cut it to five. Another triple fifty seconds later. Then, a third — all within two minutes.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Closing In: </strong>Curry and Gui Santos (20 pts) added two more buckets, trimming the deficit to one with 2:12 left</li>
<li><strong>Then, Horford Hit </strong><strong><em>Again:</em></strong> It was his fourth 3 in 3:35 of game time – and the Warriors’ first lead since 8:11 in the 2nd quarter, up two with 2:12 left</li>
<li><strong>But It Was Short Lived:</strong> LA quickly answered at the line, tying the game with under two to play</li>
</ul>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b2d541149b0065aea4a6/original.jpg?1776333524" alt="Stephen Curry" width="720" height="480"></p>
<p><strong>The Warriors</strong> needed one more bucket.</p>
<p><strong>So they went to Steph.</strong></p>
<p><strong>With all five defenders</strong> keyed on him, Curry gave it up to Draymond Green at the top — then got it right back on a dribble handoff. He looked to turn the corner, but Brook Lopez walled him off, so he stepped back and fired:</p>
<p><strong><em>“</em></strong><a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://x.com/NBA/status/2044637605329822094?lid=0qy0d1atx74x"><strong><em>Gooooooooood!</em></strong></a><strong><em>”</em></strong></p>
<p><strong>Prime’s Kevin Harlan</strong> was in disbelief. The Intuit Dome was in disarray. Curry was in the front row, watching it drop.</p>
<p><strong>Warriors up three</strong> with 50 seconds left.</p>
<p><strong>Two steals</strong> from Green later, and Golden State was golden in the Play-In once again.</p>
<hr>
<h3>2. WARRIORS’ WAY: VETERAN CHAMPS LIFT GOLDEN STATE LATE</h3>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b2fca14ce30064c0e41c/original.jpg?1776333563" alt="Stephen Curry, Draymond Green" width="576" height="720"></p>
<p><strong>When the lights</strong> turn up, Steph Curry shines brightest.</p>
<p><strong>Exactly one year</strong> from last night, on April 15, 2025, Curry scored 15 of his 37 points in the 4th quarter of a Play-In game to beat Memphis, sending Golden State to the Playoffs.</p>
<p><strong>That’s what the four-time</strong> NBA champion does. Tenth in all-time Playoff points and first in Playoff 3s, Curry’s built for moments like these. Late, tight, season on the line — where time and time again, Curry delivers.</p>
<p><strong>And he’s owned</strong> the SoFi Play-In stage.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Meeting The Moment:</strong> Steph has played in five career Play-In games. He’s scored 35+ points in four of them</li>
<li><strong>Another Level:</strong> Only one other player has multiple career 35+ point performances in the Play-In: Ja Morant, with two</li>
<li><strong>Always Dangerous:</strong> Curry also owns the highest scoring average in Play-In Tournament history (min. 3 GP), with a scorching 34 ppg</li>
<li><strong>Unfazed Fire:</strong> That included playing past a minutes restriction last night as he works his way back from a knee injury, making multiple trips to the locker room before taking over</li>
<li><strong>That Takeover? </strong>Seven 3s on 12-of-23 shooting, including a 10-of-14 mark in the 2nd half – capped by another clutch Curry banger</li>
<li><strong>“All it takes is one,”</strong> said Steve Kerr postgame of Curry’s outbursts. “That’s his magic. He can will himself into a game. He has so much belief, so much competitive desire – it’s amazing to watch.”</li>
</ul>
<p><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b37476a86700729c3c18/original.gif?1776333683" alt="Al Horford, Draymond Green" width="600" height="600"></p>
<p><strong>That competitive desire</strong> fuels a veteran-laden Warriors squad with championship DNA – and it showed up Wednesday.</p>
<p><strong>It started </strong>with Al Horford.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Worth The Wait:</strong> Horford, who won the 2024 NBA title with Boston, has battled through injuries in his first season with Golden State, missing 37 games</li>
<li><strong>But he saved</strong> his biggest game for the biggest spot, scoring 12 in the 4th with his four late 3s – matching his highest-scoring quarter of the season</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>The night ended</strong> with Draymond Green.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Winning Hustle:</strong> After Curry’s go-ahead 3, Green stole LA’s inbound to set up a Brandin Podziemski bucket</li>
<li><strong>10 Seconds Later:</strong> “<a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://x.com/NBA/status/2044640690320097605?lid=cui4zp47m3hn">Green stole the ball</a>!” Another swipe, this time on Kawhi Leonard (21 pts, 7 ast), who Green limited to 2 points in the 4th</li>
<li><strong>Winning Duo:</strong> Draymond finished with 7 points, 6 boards, 9 assists and a game-high 4 steals, as he and Curry reached the Playoffs together for the 11th time</li>
<li><strong>“I’ve been watching them</strong> for 12 years,” said Kerr of Curry and Green. “There’s a reason we have four championships, and it’s the competitiveness. The heart. The will…</li>
<li><strong>“It’s been such a tough year, </strong>but to just show what they’re made of – what we’re made of … with all the wins we’ve ever had here … this one is right up there.”</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Up Next?</strong> Another elimination game, as the Warriors visit the Suns on Friday (10 ET) to decide the West’s No. 8 seed in the Playoffs. The loser goes home.</p>
<hr>
<h3>3. SIXERS CLAIM NO. 7 SEED: MAXEY MOVES PHILLY PAST ORLANDO</h3>
<p><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b41b30c5400064585aa3/original.gif?1776333851" alt="Adem Bona, Tyrese Maxey" width="600" height="600"></p>
<p><strong>A year </strong>with its share of key injuries and absences.</p>
<p><strong>An emergency</strong> appendectomy for franchise star Joel Embiid.</p>
<p><strong>A Play-In elimination</strong> setting promising the unpredictable.</p>
<p><strong>Questions continued</strong> to follow the 76ers and their 2025-26 aspirations through the finish of the regular season.</p>
<p><strong>Wednesday night</strong>, Tyrese Maxey had an answer.</p>
<p><strong>In front of </strong><a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://x.com/NBA/status/2044578069571104812?s=20&lid=2szdmvqpmrt5"><strong>Allen Iverson</strong> and the Philly faithful</a> and in the classic black jerseys the team wore in the 2001 Finals, Maxey drove the Sixers back to the Playoffs after missing out last year.</p>
<p><strong>Sixers 109, Magic 97:</strong> Maxey scored 11 of his 31 points in the 4th quarter, including a stretch of 7 straight that pulled Philly out of a one-possession game to a double-digit win, claiming the No. 7 seed for the Sixers and sending them to a First-Round series in Boston.</p>
<p><strong>Desmond Bane’s</strong> game-high 34 points led the Magic, who now return home to host the 9-seed Hornets in the SoFi Play-In Tournament finale Friday to decide the 8-seed. | <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://www.nba.com/game/orl-vs-phi-0052500101?lid=xlou8jq0eaij">Recap</a></p>
<p><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b4569dca820064a955b4/original.gif?1776333910" alt="Tyrese Maxey" width="600" height="600"></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>“I told [the team] at the beginning of the year</strong>, ‘I don’t care what it takes, we’re going to the Playoffs,’” Maxey said postgame</li>
<li><strong>What It Took:</strong> Tyrese got his 31 efficiently but on a game-high 25 shots (44 FG%), leading all players with 42 minutes, following a season where he played the most minutes of his career (38 mpg)</li>
<li><strong>Maxey’s Move: </strong>After a 24-14 Magic run made it 86-87 with under 8 minutes to go, Tyrese took off with a floater, a triple and another floater to stretch the lead back to 8</li>
<li><strong>The Close: </strong>Orlando never got back within 4, with Maxey rising up for a game-sealing block in the final minute, before Andre Drummond (14 pts, 10 reb) delivered the dagger 3</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>“He’s been doing a bit of that lately</strong>… understanding we need his greatness at the right time,” coach Nick Nurse said of Maxey and his decisive 7-point burst.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Top-5 Ty:</strong> Maxey’s 31 is good for the 5th-highest scoring performance of this year’s Play-In Tournament, after ranking top-5 in scoring (career-best 28.3 ppg) throughout a season in which all his offensive averages increased</li>
<li><strong>Winning Wings:</strong> Joining Maxey with over 40 minutes played Wednesday, VJ Edgecombe (11 reb) and Kelly Oubre Jr. (5 3s) contributed 19 points apiece</li>
<li><strong>What’s Next: </strong>The 7-seed Sixers now face the 2-seed Celtics Sunday, the start of an NBA-record 23rd Playoff series between the two teams. No other postseason matchup has met more than 15 times</li>
</ul>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b4c314f8980064a393da/original.jpg?1776334018" alt="Orlando Magic" width="720" height="576"></p>
<p><strong>The Magic</strong> still have another chance to join the Sixers in that Playoff field, facing a Hornets squad that’s won its way into the Play-In finale, and six of its last eight games.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>“There’s frustration, but it’s also a great opportunity</strong> ahead of us on Friday,” Bane said. “Get the chance to play at home in front of our fans… and the chance to go clinch a Playoff spot. I’m excited for the challenge.”</li>
<li><strong>Orlando’s Outputs:</strong> After Bane’s 34 on Wednesday, the Magic got a combined 43 points from Paolo Banchero (18), Anthony Black (13) and Franz Wagner (12)</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h3>4. FRIDAY LOOK-AHEAD: WIN OR GO HOME ON PRIME</h3>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0ba09efaf290064d3f7fe/original.jpg?1776335368" alt="Stephen Curry, Miles Bridges" width="720" height="500"></p>
<p><strong>After two nights</strong> of 7 vs. 8 and 9 vs. 10 Play-In games, the stakes take another step up in Friday’s Play-In finale.</p>
<p><strong>Win?</strong> A date with the No. 1 seed in the NBA Playoffs.</p>
<p><strong>Lose?</strong> That’s a wrap on 2025-26.</p>
<p><strong>We’ll dive more</strong> into each matchup in Friday’s edition, but let’s get you started here…</p>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b5594463140064ce09ab/original.jpg?1776334169" alt="Kon Knueppel, Desmond Bane" width="624" height="456"></p>
<p><strong>East | No. 10 Hornets at No. 8 Magic (7:30 ET, Prime):</strong> LaMelo Ball, Brandon Miller and Kon Knueppel lead Charlotte against Paolo Banchero, Desmond Bane and Franz Wagner in a battle of opposing styles.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>3s Are Key:</strong> While Charlotte led the regular season in made 3s (16.4), no team allowed fewer triples than Orlando, which held its opponents to just 12.1 per game</li>
<li><strong>Late Magic: </strong>Although the Magic couldn’t edge Philly last night, they’ve shined in crunch time this season with 21 clutch wins – tied with the Pistons for the most in the NBA</li>
<li><strong>Buzzing Hornets: </strong>They’ll now face a Hornets squad coming off an <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://x.com/NBA/status/2044241389815279687?lid=xnbjggf47j75">electric OT win</a> over Miami in Tuesday’s winner-take-all duel – moving them one win shy of their first Playoff berth in a decade</li>
<li><strong>Up Next: </strong>The winner earns the East’s No. 8 seed, setting up a First Round matchup against the No. 1 Pistons (Game 1: Sunday at 6:30 ET, NBC)</li>
</ul>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b58938ecca00640c1146/original.jpg?1776334216" alt="Stephen Curry, Devin Booker" width="620" height="471"></p>
<p><strong>West | No. 10 Warriors at No. 7 Suns (10 ET, Prime):</strong> Steph, Draymond & Co. look to complete their climb against Devin Booker, Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Series Rewind: </strong>Golden State took three of four from Phoenix this season, with the Suns earning a last-minute 99-98 win Dec. 18 in which they held Curry to 2-of-9 from 3</li>
<li><strong>Three & D Edge:</strong> Phoenix (45-37) finished 8 games ahead of Golden State and joined Boston as the only teams to rank top-5 in 3s (14.8 3pg) and top-10 in defense (112.9)</li>
<li><strong>More History?</strong> The Dubs aim for a second road win to join last year’s Miami Heat as the only 10-seeds to advance out of the SoFi Play-In Tournament</li>
<li><strong>OKC Awaits:</strong> The West’s No. 8 seed goes to the winner, sending them into the First Round against the defending-champion 1-seed Thunder (Game 1: Sunday at 3:30 ET, ABC)</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h3>5. AWAITING FRIDAY’S WINNERS: WHAT LIES AHEAD FOR THE NO. 1 SEEDS</h3>
<p><img decoding="async" src="https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0325130c5400064583318/original.jpg?1776300625" alt="Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Cade Cunningham" width="720" height="480"></p>
<p><strong>Ask the last three </strong>NBA champions:</p>
<p><strong>2025: No. 1-seed </strong>Thunder.</p>
<p><strong>2024: No. 1-seed </strong>Celtics.</p>
<p><strong>2023: No. 1-seed </strong>Nuggets.</p>
<p><strong>The top seed</strong> in the Playoffs is a coveted spot to be in. But what are some of the challenges?</p>
<p><strong>This year’s Thunder and Pistons, </strong>for instance, won’t have their First Round Playoff matchups solidified until Friday’s Play-In finale — making them the last seeds to have their opponent set.</p>
<p><strong>NBA.com’s Shaun Powell</strong> examines the potential traps and hurdles OKC and Detroit could face on the Playoff road ahead, as they prepare for their Play-In challengers:</p>
<p><strong><em>Being a top seed</em></strong><em> in the NBA playoffs has its privileges. Home-court advantage throughout the rounds and the most favorable matchups every round from a seeding standpoint are the obvious two…</em></p>
<p><strong><em>[But] in the Playoffs</em></strong><em>, even with a seven-game series, sometimes the hotter team ultimately prevails, making all that hard work in the regular season moot. </em></p>
<p><strong><em>These are the realities</em> </strong><em>for the top-seeded Detroit Pistons and Oklahoma City Thunder starting next weekend.” </em>| <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://www.nba.com/news/pistons-thunder-top-seeds-playoffs?lid=dqc6xzmcujhy">Read More</a></p>

Staff","firstName":"","lastName":"","organization":"","isNBAStaff":false,"authorPhoto":"","socialHandle":"","socialLink":"","wpseoTitle":"","wpseoMetadesc":"","description":"","userUrl":"","featuredAuthor":false},"contentText":"A golden comeback, capped by an instant-classic Steph Curry triple, in another Play-In thriller.\r\n\r\nAs coach Steve Kerr put it: 'This is why Steph came back.'\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n5 STORIES IN TODAY'S EDITION 🏀\r\nDubs Stun Clips: Curry’s vintage flurry, Horford’s epic 4th and an unreal clutch rally\r\n\r\nWarriors' Way: With their season on the line, Golden State’s championship vets delivered\r\n\r\nPlayoff Philly: Key contributions and Maxey’s 31 points move Sixers past Magic\r\n\r\nFriday Look-Ahead: Magic-Hornets, Warriors-Suns, winner to Playoffs, loser goes home\r\n\r\nAwaiting Friday’s Winners: Shaun Powell examines what’s ahead for both No. 1 seeds\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nBUT FIRST ... ⏰\r\n\r\n\r\nThe SoFi NBA Play-In Tournament sends its final two teams to the Playoffs – and two more home – on Friday, when the Magic host the Hornets (7:30 ET, Prime), followed by the Warriors visiting the Suns (10 ET, Prime).\r\n\r\nPlayoffs Saturday: Friday’s winners earn their conference’s No. 8 seed (but won't play until Sunday). The NBA Playoffs tip off Saturday with four Game 1s across Prime and ABC, followed by four more Sunday on ABC and NBC.\r\n\r\nViewership Jumps: 170 million people in the U.S. watched NBA games across ABC/ESPN, Amazon Prime Video, NBC/Peacock and NBA TV during the regular season – the most in 24 years and up 86% from last season.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n1. CURRY IGNITES WARRIORS PAST CLIPS IN ANOTHER PLAY-IN CLASSIC\r\n\r\n\r\nThey had lost seven of their last eight games entering Wednesday.\r\n\r\nThey were 8-33 this year when trailing entering the 4th quarter.\r\n\r\nBut with their season on the brink, staring at a 13-point deficit with 9:53 left in last night’s win-or-go-home showdown, the Warriors found a way.\r\n\r\nAnd in the biggest moment, it was Steph Curry.\r\n\r\nWarriors 126, Clippers 121: Curry (7 3s) dropped 27 of his 35 points in the 2nd half, willing Golden State back in the 4th alongside Al Horford (14 pts, 3 3s) as the Warriors closed on a 16-5 run, highlighted by Steph’s off-balance, go-ahead 3 with 50 seconds left to stun the Clippers. | Recap\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nSteph Spark: After going down 10 early in the 2nd half, Curry caught fire for 16 points in the 3rd, drilling three triples in a three-minute span to cut the deficit to six entering the 4th.\r\n\r\nIt was only the start.\r\n\r\n \tClips Clap Back: After holding multiple double-digit leads, Bennedict Mathurin (23 pts) and Darius Garland (21 pts, 8 ast) pushed LA’s edge back to 13 with 9:53\r\n \tSteph Says Hello: Moments later, Curry checked back in and immediately splashed a 3, jumpstarting a push that trimmed the deficit to seven\r\n\r\nThen, Al’s Eruption: With the comeback brewing, Horford brought the jolt: a 3 to cut it to five. Another triple fifty seconds later. Then, a third — all within two minutes.\r\n\r\n \tClosing In: Curry and Gui Santos (20 pts) added two more buckets, trimming the deficit to one with 2:12 left\r\n \tThen, Horford Hit Again: It was his fourth 3 in 3:35 of game time – and the Warriors’ first lead since 8:11 in the 2nd quarter, up two with 2:12 left\r\n \tBut It Was Short Lived: LA quickly answered at the line, tying the game with under two to play\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nThe Warriors needed one more bucket.\r\n\r\nSo they went to Steph.\r\n\r\nWith all five defenders keyed on him, Curry gave it up to Draymond Green at the top — then got it right back on a dribble handoff. He looked to turn the corner, but Brook Lopez walled him off, so he stepped back and fired:\r\n\r\n“Gooooooooood!”\r\n\r\nPrime’s Kevin Harlan was in disbelief. The Intuit Dome was in disarray. Curry was in the front row, watching it drop.\r\n\r\nWarriors up three with 50 seconds left.\r\n\r\nTwo steals from Green later, and Golden State was golden in the Play-In once again.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n2. WARRIORS' WAY: VETERAN CHAMPS LIFT GOLDEN STATE LATE\r\n\r\n\r\nWhen the lights turn up, Steph Curry shines brightest.\r\n\r\nExactly one year from last night, on April 15, 2025, Curry scored 15 of his 37 points in the 4th quarter of a Play-In game to beat Memphis, sending Golden State to the Playoffs.\r\n\r\nThat’s what the four-time NBA champion does. Tenth in all-time Playoff points and first in Playoff 3s, Curry’s built for moments like these. Late, tight, season on the line — where time and time again, Curry delivers.\r\n\r\nAnd he’s owned the SoFi Play-In stage.\r\n\r\n \tMeeting The Moment: Steph has played in five career Play-In games. He’s scored 35+ points in four of them\r\n \tAnother Level: Only one other player has multiple career 35+ point performances in the Play-In: Ja Morant, with two\r\n \tAlways Dangerous: Curry also owns the highest scoring average in Play-In Tournament history (min. 3 GP), with a scorching 34 ppg\r\n \tUnfazed Fire: That included playing past a minutes restriction last night as he works his way back from a knee injury, making multiple trips to the locker room before taking over\r\n \tThat Takeover? Seven 3s on 12-of-23 shooting, including a 10-of-14 mark in the 2nd half – capped by another clutch Curry banger\r\n \t“All it takes is one,” said Steve Kerr postgame of Curry’s outbursts. “That’s his magic. He can will himself into a game. He has so much belief, so much competitive desire – it’s amazing to watch.”\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nThat competitive desire fuels a veteran-laden Warriors squad with championship DNA – and it showed up Wednesday.\r\n\r\nIt started with Al Horford.\r\n\r\n \tWorth The Wait: Horford, who won the 2024 NBA title with Boston, has battled through injuries in his first season with Golden State, missing 37 games\r\n \tBut he saved his biggest game for the biggest spot, scoring 12 in the 4th with his four late 3s – matching his highest-scoring quarter of the season\r\n\r\nThe night ended with Draymond Green.\r\n\r\n \tWinning Hustle: After Curry’s go-ahead 3, Green stole LA’s inbound to set up a Brandin Podziemski bucket\r\n \t10 Seconds Later: “Green stole the ball!” Another swipe, this time on Kawhi Leonard (21 pts, 7 ast), who Green limited to 2 points in the 4th\r\n \tWinning Duo: Draymond finished with 7 points, 6 boards, 9 assists and a game-high 4 steals, as he and Curry reached the Playoffs together for the 11th time\r\n \t“I’ve been watching them for 12 years,” said Kerr of Curry and Green. “There’s a reason we have four championships, and it’s the competitiveness. The heart. The will…\r\n \t“It's been such a tough year, but to just show what they're made of – what we're made of … with all the wins we’ve ever had here … this one is right up there.”\r\n\r\nUp Next? Another elimination game, as the Warriors visit the Suns on Friday (10 ET) to decide the West’s No. 8 seed in the Playoffs. The loser goes home.\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n3. SIXERS CLAIM NO. 7 SEED: MAXEY MOVES PHILLY PAST ORLANDO\r\n\r\n\r\nA year with its share of key injuries and absences.\r\n\r\nAn emergency appendectomy for franchise star Joel Embiid.\r\n\r\nA Play-In elimination setting promising the unpredictable.\r\n\r\nQuestions continued to follow the 76ers and their 2025-26 aspirations through the finish of the regular season.\r\n\r\nWednesday night, Tyrese Maxey had an answer.\r\n\r\nIn front of Allen Iverson and the Philly faithful and in the classic black jerseys the team wore in the 2001 Finals, Maxey drove the Sixers back to the Playoffs after missing out last year.\r\n\r\nSixers 109, Magic 97: Maxey scored 11 of his 31 points in the 4th quarter, including a stretch of 7 straight that pulled Philly out of a one-possession game to a double-digit win, claiming the No. 7 seed for the Sixers and sending them to a First-Round series in Boston.\r\n\r\nDesmond Bane’s game-high 34 points led the Magic, who now return home to host the 9-seed Hornets in the SoFi Play-In Tournament finale Friday to decide the 8-seed. | Recap\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n \t“I told [the team] at the beginning of the year, ‘I don’t care what it takes, we’re going to the Playoffs,’” Maxey said postgame\r\n \tWhat It Took: Tyrese got his 31 efficiently but on a game-high 25 shots (44 FG%), leading all players with 42 minutes, following a season where he played the most minutes of his career (38 mpg)\r\n \tMaxey’s Move: After a 24-14 Magic run made it 86-87 with under 8 minutes to go, Tyrese took off with a floater, a triple and another floater to stretch the lead back to 8\r\n \tThe Close: Orlando never got back within 4, with Maxey rising up for a game-sealing block in the final minute, before Andre Drummond (14 pts, 10 reb) delivered the dagger 3\r\n\r\n“He’s been doing a bit of that lately… understanding we need his greatness at the right time,” coach Nick Nurse said of Maxey and his decisive 7-point burst.\r\n\r\n \tTop-5 Ty: Maxey’s 31 is good for the 5th-highest scoring performance of this year’s Play-In Tournament, after ranking top-5 in scoring (career-best 28.3 ppg) throughout a season in which all his offensive averages increased\r\n \tWinning Wings: Joining Maxey with over 40 minutes played Wednesday, VJ Edgecombe (11 reb) and Kelly Oubre Jr. (5 3s) contributed 19 points apiece\r\n \tWhat’s Next: The 7-seed Sixers now face the 2-seed Celtics Sunday, the start of an NBA-record 23rd Playoff series between the two teams. No other postseason matchup has met more than 15 times\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nThe Magic still have another chance to join the Sixers in that Playoff field, facing a Hornets squad that’s won its way into the Play-In finale, and six of its last eight games.\r\n\r\n \t“There’s frustration, but it’s also a great opportunity ahead of us on Friday,” Bane said. “Get the chance to play at home in front of our fans… and the chance to go clinch a Playoff spot. I’m excited for the challenge.”\r\n \tOrlando’s Outputs: After Bane’s 34 on Wednesday, the Magic got a combined 43 points from Paolo Banchero (18), Anthony Black (13) and Franz Wagner (12)\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n4. FRIDAY LOOK-AHEAD: WIN OR GO HOME ON PRIME\r\n\r\n\r\nAfter two nights of 7 vs. 8 and 9 vs. 10 Play-In games, the stakes take another step up in Friday’s Play-In finale.\r\n\r\nWin? A date with the No. 1 seed in the NBA Playoffs.\r\n\r\nLose? That’s a wrap on 2025-26.\r\n\r\nWe’ll dive more into each matchup in Friday’s edition, but let’s get you started here…\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nEast | No. 10 Hornets at No. 8 Magic (7:30 ET, Prime): LaMelo Ball, Brandon Miller and Kon Knueppel lead Charlotte against Paolo Banchero, Desmond Bane and Franz Wagner in a battle of opposing styles.\r\n\r\n \t3s Are Key: While Charlotte led the regular season in made 3s (16.4), no team allowed fewer triples than Orlando, which held its opponents to just 12.1 per game\r\n \tLate Magic: Although the Magic couldn’t edge Philly last night, they’ve shined in crunch time this season with 21 clutch wins – tied with the Pistons for the most in the NBA\r\n \tBuzzing Hornets: They’ll now face a Hornets squad coming off an electric OT win over Miami in Tuesday’s winner-take-all duel – moving them one win shy of their first Playoff berth in a decade\r\n \tUp Next: The winner earns the East’s No. 8 seed, setting up a First Round matchup against the No. 1 Pistons (Game 1: Sunday at 6:30 ET, NBC)\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nWest | No. 10 Warriors at No. 7 Suns (10 ET, Prime): Steph, Draymond \u0026amp; Co. look to complete their climb against Devin Booker, Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks.\r\n\r\n \tSeries Rewind: Golden State took three of four from Phoenix this season, with the Suns earning a last-minute 99-98 win Dec. 18 in which they held Curry to 2-of-9 from 3\r\n \tThree \u0026amp; D Edge: Phoenix (45-37) finished 8 games ahead of Golden State and joined Boston as the only teams to rank top-5 in 3s (14.8 3pg) and top-10 in defense (112.9)\r\n \tMore History? The Dubs aim for a second road win to join last year’s Miami Heat as the only 10-seeds to advance out of the SoFi Play-In Tournament\r\n \tOKC Awaits: The West’s No. 8 seed goes to the winner, sending them into the First Round against the defending-champion 1-seed Thunder (Game 1: Sunday at 3:30 ET, ABC)\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n5. AWAITING FRIDAY’S WINNERS: WHAT LIES AHEAD FOR THE NO. 1 SEEDS\r\n\r\n\r\nAsk the last three NBA champions:\r\n\r\n2025: No. 1-seed Thunder.\r\n\r\n2024: No. 1-seed Celtics.\r\n\r\n2023: No. 1-seed Nuggets.\r\n\r\nThe top seed in the Playoffs is a coveted spot to be in. But what are some of the challenges?\r\n\r\nThis year’s Thunder and Pistons, for instance, won’t have their First Round Playoff matchups solidified until Friday’s Play-In finale -- making them the last seeds to have their opponent set.\r\n\r\nNBA.com’s Shaun Powell examines the potential traps and hurdles OKC and Detroit could face on the Playoff road ahead, as they prepare for their Play-In challengers:\r\n\r\nBeing a top seed in the NBA playoffs has its privileges. Home-court advantage throughout the rounds and the most favorable matchups every round from a seeding standpoint are the obvious two…\r\n\r\n[But] in the Playoffs, even with a seven-game series, sometimes the hotter team ultimately prevails, making all that hard work in the regular season moot. \r\n\r\nThese are the realities for the top-seeded Detroit Pistons and Oklahoma City Thunder starting next weekend.” | Read More\r\n\r\nWant to share Starting 5 with a friend? Send them this link.\r\n\r\nShape the Starting 5.  Email us here.\r\n\r\nDon't have the NBA App? Way:\u003c/strong\u003e With their season on the line, Golden State’s championship vets delivered\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePlayoff Philly:\u003c/strong\u003e Key contributions and Maxey’s 31 points move Sixers past Magic\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFriday Look-Ahead:\u003c/strong\u003e Magic-Hornets, Warriors-Suns, winner to Playoffs, loser goes home\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAwaiting Friday’s Winners:\u003c/strong\u003e Shaun Powell examines what’s ahead for both No. 1 seeds\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cspan class=\"tinyMce-placeholder\"\u003eBUT FIRST \u0026#8230; ⏰\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b168aed2580064d1d9f7/original.jpg?1776333159\" alt=\"Scores \u0026amp; Schedule\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThe \u003c/strong\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://www.nba.com/news/nba-play-in-tournament?lid=h145xryg2hdy\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSoFi NBA Play-In Tournament\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e sends its final two teams to the Playoffs – and two more home – on Friday, when the Magic host the Hornets (\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://www.nba.com/game/0052500201?lid=kqx27qfma00f\"\u003e7:30 ET, Prime\u003c/a\u003e), followed by the Warriors visiting the Suns (\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://www.nba.com/game/0052500211?lid=iq77ngoi8q56\"\u003e10 ET, Prime\u003c/a\u003e).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePlayoffs Saturday: \u003c/strong\u003eFriday’s winners earn their conference’s No. 8 seed (but won\u0026#8217;t play until Sunday). The \u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://www.nba.com/playoffs/2026?lid=n825pd4efzjz\"\u003eNBA Playoffs\u003c/a\u003e tip off Saturday with four Game 1s across Prime and ABC, followed by four more Sunday on ABC and NBC.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eViewership Jumps: \u003c/strong\u003e170 million people in the U.S. watched NBA games across ABC/ESPN, Amazon Prime Video, NBC/Peacock and NBA TV during the regular season – \u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://x.com/NBA/status/2044447933529989544?lid=yfd32u2a6gds\"\u003ethe most in 24 years\u003c/a\u003e and up 86% from last season.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b18617cd200064855548/original.jpg?1776333190\" alt=\"Playoff Picture\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e1. CURRY IGNITES WARRIORS PAST CLIPS IN ANOTHER PLAY-IN CLASSIC\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b9f77eac8200644b5105/original.jpg?1776335350\" alt=\"Stephen Curry\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThey had lost seven\u003c/strong\u003e of their last eight games entering Wednesday.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThey were 8-33\u003c/strong\u003e this year when trailing entering the 4th quarter.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBut with their season\u003c/strong\u003e on the brink, staring at a 13-point deficit with 9:53 left in last night’s win-or-go-home showdown, the Warriors found a way.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAnd in the biggest moment,\u003c/strong\u003e it was Steph Curry.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWarriors 126, Clippers 121:\u003c/strong\u003e Curry (7 3s) dropped 27 of his \u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://x.com/NBA/status/2044645883568890095?lid=gcd2j1vl14yw\"\u003e35 points\u003c/a\u003e in the 2nd half, willing Golden State back in the 4th alongside Al Horford (14 pts, 3 3s) as the Warriors closed on a 16-5 run, highlighted by Steph’s off-balance, go-ahead 3 with 50 seconds left to stun the Clippers. | \u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://www.nba.com/game/gsw-vs-lac-0052500131?lid=fyoh79l4mvhl\"\u003eRecap\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b28df1ba0e0065cb14d8/original.jpg?1776333452\" alt=\"Al Horford, Stephen Curry\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSteph Spark:\u003c/strong\u003e After going down 10 early in the 2nd half, Curry caught fire for 16 points in the 3rd, drilling three triples in a three-minute span to cut the deficit to six entering the 4th.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIt was only\u003c/strong\u003e the start.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eClips Clap Back:\u003c/strong\u003e After holding multiple double-digit leads, Bennedict Mathurin (23 pts) and Darius Garland (21 pts, 8 ast) pushed LA’s edge back to 13 with 9:53\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSteph Says Hello:\u003c/strong\u003e Moments later, Curry checked back in and immediately splashed a 3, jumpstarting a push that trimmed the deficit to seven\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThen, Al’s Eruption: \u003c/strong\u003eWith the comeback brewing, Horford brought the jolt: a 3 to cut it to five. Another triple fifty seconds later. Then, a third — all within two minutes.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eClosing In: \u003c/strong\u003eCurry and Gui Santos (20 pts) added two more buckets, trimming the deficit to one with 2:12 left\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThen, Horford Hit \u003c/strong\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003cem\u003eAgain:\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e It was his fourth 3 in 3:35 of game time – and the Warriors’ first lead since 8:11 in the 2nd quarter, up two with 2:12 left\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBut It Was Short Lived:\u003c/strong\u003e LA quickly answered at the line, tying the game with under two to play\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b2d541149b0065aea4a6/original.jpg?1776333524\" alt=\"Stephen Curry\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThe Warriors\u003c/strong\u003e needed one more bucket.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSo they went to Steph.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWith all five defenders\u003c/strong\u003e keyed on him, Curry gave it up to Draymond Green at the top — then got it right back on a dribble handoff. He looked to turn the corner, but Brook Lopez walled him off, so he stepped back and fired:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003cem\u003e“\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://x.com/NBA/status/2044637605329822094?lid=0qy0d1atx74x\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003cem\u003eGooooooooood!\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003cem\u003e”\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePrime’s Kevin Harlan\u003c/strong\u003e was in disbelief. The Intuit Dome was in disarray. Curry was in the front row, watching it drop.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWarriors up three\u003c/strong\u003e with 50 seconds left.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTwo steals\u003c/strong\u003e from Green later, and Golden State was golden in the Play-In once again.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e2. WARRIORS\u0026#8217; WAY: VETERAN CHAMPS LIFT GOLDEN STATE LATE\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b2fca14ce30064c0e41c/original.jpg?1776333563\" alt=\"Stephen Curry, Draymond Green\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhen the lights\u003c/strong\u003e turn up, Steph Curry shines brightest.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eExactly one year\u003c/strong\u003e from last night, on April 15, 2025, Curry scored 15 of his 37 points in the 4th quarter of a Play-In game to beat Memphis, sending Golden State to the Playoffs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThat’s what the four-time\u003c/strong\u003e NBA champion does. Tenth in all-time Playoff points and first in Playoff 3s, Curry’s built for moments like these. Late, tight, season on the line — where time and time again, Curry delivers.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAnd he’s owned\u003c/strong\u003e the SoFi Play-In stage.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eMeeting The Moment:\u003c/strong\u003e Steph has played in five career Play-In games. He’s scored 35+ points in four of them\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAnother Level:\u003c/strong\u003e Only one other player has multiple career 35+ point performances in the Play-In: Ja Morant, with two\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAlways Dangerous:\u003c/strong\u003e Curry also owns the highest scoring average in Play-In Tournament history (min. 3 GP), with a scorching 34 ppg\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUnfazed Fire:\u003c/strong\u003e That included playing past a minutes restriction last night as he works his way back from a knee injury, making multiple trips to the locker room before taking over\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThat Takeover? \u003c/strong\u003eSeven 3s on 12-of-23 shooting, including a 10-of-14 mark in the 2nd half – capped by another clutch Curry banger\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e“All it takes is one,”\u003c/strong\u003e said Steve Kerr postgame of Curry’s outbursts. “That’s his magic. He can will himself into a game. He has so much belief, so much competitive desire – it’s amazing to watch.”\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b37476a86700729c3c18/original.gif?1776333683\" alt=\"Al Horford, Draymond Green\" width=\"600\" height=\"600\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThat competitive desire\u003c/strong\u003e fuels a veteran-laden Warriors squad with championship DNA – and it showed up Wednesday.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIt started \u003c/strong\u003ewith Al Horford.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWorth The Wait:\u003c/strong\u003e Horford, who won the 2024 NBA title with Boston, has battled through injuries in his first season with Golden State, missing 37 games\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBut he saved\u003c/strong\u003e his biggest game for the biggest spot, scoring 12 in the 4th with his four late 3s – matching his highest-scoring quarter of the season\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThe night ended\u003c/strong\u003e with Draymond Green.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWinning Hustle:\u003c/strong\u003e After Curry’s go-ahead 3, Green stole LA’s inbound to set up a Brandin Podziemski bucket\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e10 Seconds Later:\u003c/strong\u003e “\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://x.com/NBA/status/2044640690320097605?lid=cui4zp47m3hn\"\u003eGreen stole the ball\u003c/a\u003e!” Another swipe, this time on Kawhi Leonard (21 pts, 7 ast), who Green limited to 2 points in the 4th\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWinning Duo:\u003c/strong\u003e Draymond finished with 7 points, 6 boards, 9 assists and a game-high 4 steals, as he and Curry reached the Playoffs together for the 11th time\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e“I’ve been watching them\u003c/strong\u003e for 12 years,” said Kerr of Curry and Green. “There’s a reason we have four championships, and it’s the competitiveness. The heart. The will…\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e“It\u0026#8217;s been such a tough year, \u003c/strong\u003ebut to just show what they\u0026#8217;re made of – what we\u0026#8217;re made of … with all the wins we’ve ever had here … this one is right up there.”\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUp Next?\u003c/strong\u003e Another elimination game, as the Warriors visit the Suns on Friday (10 ET) to decide the West’s No. 8 seed in the Playoffs. The loser goes home.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e3. SIXERS CLAIM NO. 7 SEED: MAXEY MOVES PHILLY PAST ORLANDO\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b41b30c5400064585aa3/original.gif?1776333851\" alt=\"Adem Bona, Tyrese Maxey\" width=\"600\" height=\"600\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA year \u003c/strong\u003ewith its share of key injuries and absences.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAn emergency\u003c/strong\u003e appendectomy for franchise star Joel Embiid.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA Play-In elimination\u003c/strong\u003e setting promising the unpredictable.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQuestions continued\u003c/strong\u003e to follow the 76ers and their 2025-26 aspirations through the finish of the regular season.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWednesday night\u003c/strong\u003e, Tyrese Maxey had an answer.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIn front of \u003c/strong\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://x.com/NBA/status/2044578069571104812?s=20\u0026amp;lid=2szdmvqpmrt5\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAllen Iverson\u003c/strong\u003e and the Philly faithful\u003c/a\u003e and in the classic black jerseys the team wore in the 2001 Finals, Maxey drove the Sixers back to the Playoffs after missing out last year.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSixers 109, Magic 97:\u003c/strong\u003e Maxey scored 11 of his 31 points in the 4th quarter, including a stretch of 7 straight that pulled Philly out of a one-possession game to a double-digit win, claiming the No. 7 seed for the Sixers and sending them to a First-Round series in Boston.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDesmond Bane’s\u003c/strong\u003e game-high 34 points led the Magic, who now return home to host the 9-seed Hornets in the SoFi Play-In Tournament finale Friday to decide the 8-seed. | \u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://www.nba.com/game/orl-vs-phi-0052500101?lid=xlou8jq0eaij\"\u003eRecap\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazy\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b4569dca820064a955b4/original.gif?1776333910\" alt=\"Tyrese Maxey\" width=\"600\" height=\"600\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e“I told [the team] at the beginning of the year\u003c/strong\u003e, ‘I don’t care what it takes, we’re going to the Playoffs,’” Maxey said postgame\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat It Took:\u003c/strong\u003e Tyrese got his 31 efficiently but on a game-high 25 shots (44 FG%), leading all players with 42 minutes, following a season where he played the most minutes of his career (38 mpg)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eMaxey’s Move: \u003c/strong\u003eAfter a 24-14 Magic run made it 86-87 with under 8 minutes to go, Tyrese took off with a floater, a triple and another floater to stretch the lead back to 8\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThe Close: \u003c/strong\u003eOrlando never got back within 4, with Maxey rising up for a game-sealing block in the final minute, before Andre Drummond (14 pts, 10 reb) delivered the dagger 3\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e“He’s been doing a bit of that lately\u003c/strong\u003e… understanding we need his greatness at the right time,” coach Nick Nurse said of Maxey and his decisive 7-point burst.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTop-5 Ty:\u003c/strong\u003e Maxey’s 31 is good for the 5th-highest scoring performance of this year’s Play-In Tournament, after ranking top-5 in scoring (career-best 28.3 ppg) throughout a season in which all his offensive averages increased\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWinning Wings:\u003c/strong\u003e Joining Maxey with over 40 minutes played Wednesday, VJ Edgecombe (11 reb) and Kelly Oubre Jr. (5 3s) contributed 19 points apiece\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat’s Next: \u003c/strong\u003eThe 7-seed Sixers now face the 2-seed Celtics Sunday, the start of an NBA-record 23rd Playoff series between the two teams. No other postseason matchup has met more than 15 times\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b4c314f8980064a393da/original.jpg?1776334018\" alt=\"Orlando Magic\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThe Magic\u003c/strong\u003e still have another chance to join the Sixers in that Playoff field, facing a Hornets squad that’s won its way into the Play-In finale, and six of its last eight games.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e“There’s frustration, but it’s also a great opportunity\u003c/strong\u003e ahead of us on Friday,” Bane said. “Get the chance to play at home in front of our fans… and the chance to go clinch a Playoff spot. I’m excited for the challenge.”\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOrlando’s Outputs:\u003c/strong\u003e After Bane’s 34 on Wednesday, the Magic got a combined 43 points from Paolo Banchero (18), Anthony Black (13) and Franz Wagner (12)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e4. FRIDAY LOOK-AHEAD: WIN OR GO HOME ON PRIME\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0ba09efaf290064d3f7fe/original.jpg?1776335368\" alt=\"Stephen Curry, Miles Bridges\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAfter two nights\u003c/strong\u003e of 7 vs. 8 and 9 vs. 10 Play-In games, the stakes take another step up in Friday’s Play-In finale.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWin?\u003c/strong\u003e A date with the No. 1 seed in the NBA Playoffs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLose?\u003c/strong\u003e That’s a wrap on 2025-26.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWe’ll dive more\u003c/strong\u003e into each matchup in Friday’s edition, but let’s get you started here…\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b5594463140064ce09ab/original.jpg?1776334169\" alt=\"Kon Knueppel, Desmond Bane\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eEast | No. 10 Hornets at No. 8 Magic (7:30 ET, Prime):\u003c/strong\u003e LaMelo Ball, Brandon Miller and Kon Knueppel lead Charlotte against Paolo Banchero, Desmond Bane and Franz Wagner in a battle of opposing styles.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e3s Are Key:\u003c/strong\u003e While Charlotte led the regular season in made 3s (16.4), no team allowed fewer triples than Orlando, which held its opponents to just 12.1 per game\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLate Magic: \u003c/strong\u003eAlthough the Magic couldn’t edge Philly last night, they’ve shined in crunch time this season with 21 clutch wins – tied with the Pistons for the most in the NBA\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBuzzing Hornets: \u003c/strong\u003eThey’ll now face a Hornets squad coming off an \u003ca style=\"text-decoration: underline;\" href=\"https://x.com/NBA/status/2044241389815279687?lid=xnbjggf47j75\"\u003eelectric OT win\u003c/a\u003e over Miami in Tuesday’s winner-take-all duel – moving them one win shy of their first Playoff berth in a decade\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUp Next: \u003c/strong\u003eThe winner earns the East’s No. 8 seed, setting up a First Round matchup against the No. 1 Pistons (Game 1: Sunday at 6:30 ET, NBC)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0b58938ecca00640c1146/original.jpg?1776334216\" alt=\"Stephen Curry, Devin Booker\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWest | No. 10 Warriors at No. 7 Suns (10 ET, Prime):\u003c/strong\u003e Steph, Draymond \u0026amp; Co. look to complete their climb against Devin Booker, Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSeries Rewind: \u003c/strong\u003eGolden State took three of four from Phoenix this season, with the Suns earning a last-minute 99-98 win Dec. 18 in which they held Curry to 2-of-9 from 3\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThree \u0026amp; D Edge:\u003c/strong\u003e Phoenix (45-37) finished 8 games ahead of Golden State and joined Boston as the only teams to rank top-5 in 3s (14.8 3pg) and top-10 in defense (112.9)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eMore History?\u003c/strong\u003e The Dubs aim for a second road win to join last year’s Miami Heat as the only 10-seeds to advance out of the SoFi Play-In Tournament\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOKC Awaits:\u003c/strong\u003e The West’s No. 8 seed goes to the winner, sending them into the First Round against the defending-champion 1-seed Thunder (Game 1: Sunday at 3:30 ET, ABC)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003chr /\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e5. AWAITING FRIDAY’S WINNERS: WHAT LIES AHEAD FOR THE NO. 1 SEEDS\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg decoding=\"async\" src=\"https://braze-images.com/appboy/communication/assets/image_assets/images/69e0325130c5400064583318/original.jpg?1776300625\" alt=\"Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Cade Cunningham\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAsk the last three \u003c/strong\u003eNBA champions:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e2025: No. 1-seed \u003c/strong\u003eThunder.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e2024: No. 1-seed \u003c/strong\u003eCeltics.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e2023: No. 1-seed \u003c/strong\u003eNuggets.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThe top seed\u003c/strong\u003e in the Playoffs is a coveted spot to be in. But what are some of the challenges?\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThis year’s Thunder and Pistons, \u003c/strong\u003efor instance, won’t have their First Round Playoff matchups solidified until Friday’s Play-In finale \u0026#8212; making them the last seeds to have their opponent set.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNBA.com’s Shaun Powell\u003c/strong\u003e examines the potential traps and hurdles OKC and Detroit could face on the Playoff road ahead, as they prepare for their Play-In challengers:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003cem\u003eBeing a top seed\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cem\u003e in the NBA playoffs has its privileges. Home-court advantage throughout the rounds and the most favorable matchups every round from a seeding standpoint are the obvious two…\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003cem\u003e[But] in the Playoffs\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cem\u003e, even with a seven-game series, sometimes the hotter team ultimately prevails, making all that hard work in the regular season moot. \u003c/em\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003cem\u003eThese are the realities\u003c/em\u003e \u003c/strong\u003e\u003cem\u003efor the top-seeded Detroit Pistons and Oklahoma City Thunder starting next weekend.” \u003c/em\u003e| \u003ca style=\"text-decoration: gold_text:
Starting 5: Play-In thrills continue as Warriors, 76ers get wins
Stephen Curry erupts to stun Clippers, Tyrese Maxey and Sixers punch Playoff ticket and Friday’s Play-In finale is set.
A golden comeback, capped by an instant-classic Steph Curry triple, in another Play-In thriller.

As coach Steve Kerr put it: ‘This is why Steph came back.‘
5 STORIES IN TODAY’S EDITION 🏀
Dubs Stun Clips: Curry’s vintage flurry, Horford’s epic 4th and an unreal clutch rally

Warriors’ Way: With their season on the line, Golden State’s championship vets delivered

Playoff Philly: Key contributions and Maxey’s 31 points move Sixers past Magic

Friday Look-Ahead: Magic-Hornets, Warriors-Suns, winner to Playoffs, loser goes home

Awaiting Friday’s Winners: Shaun Powell examines what’s ahead for both No. 1 seeds

BUT FIRST … ⏰
The SoFi NBA Play-In Tournament sends its final two teams to the Playoffs – and two more home – on Friday, when the Magic host the Hornets (7:30 ET, Prime), followed by the Warriors visiting the Suns (10 ET, Prime).

Playoffs Saturday: Friday’s winners earn their conference’s No. 8 seed (but won’t play until Sunday). The NBA Playoffs tip off Saturday with four Game 1s across Prime and ABC, followed by four more Sunday on ABC and NBC.

Viewership Jumps: 170 million people in the U.S. watched NBA games across ABC/ESPN, Amazon Prime Video, NBC/Peacock and NBA TV during the regular season – the most in 24 years and up 86% from last season.

1. CURRY IGNITES WARRIORS PAST CLIPS IN ANOTHER PLAY-IN CLASSIC

They had lost seven of their last eight games entering Wednesday.

They were 8-33 this year when trailing entering the 4th quarter.

But with their season on the brink, staring at a 13-point deficit with 9:53 left in last night’s win-or-go-home showdown, the Warriors found a way.

And in the biggest moment, it was Steph Curry.

Warriors 126, Clippers 121: Curry (7 3s) dropped 27 of his 35 points in the 2nd half, willing Golden State back in the 4th alongside Al Horford (14 pts, 3 3s) as the Warriors closed on a 16-5 run, highlighted by Steph’s off-balance, go-ahead 3 with 50 seconds left to stun the Clippers. | Recap


Steph Spark: After going down 10 early in the 2nd half, Curry caught fire for 16 points in the 3rd, drilling three triples in a three-minute span to cut the deficit to six entering the 4th.

It was only the start.

Clips Clap Back: After holding multiple double-digit leads, Bennedict Mathurin (23 pts) and Darius Garland (21 pts, 8 ast) pushed LA’s edge back to 13 with 9:53
Steph Says Hello: Moments later, Curry checked back in and immediately splashed a 3, jumpstarting a push that trimmed the deficit to seven
Then, Al’s Eruption: With the comeback brewing, Horford brought the jolt: a 3 to cut it to five. Another triple fifty seconds later. Then, a third — all within two minutes.

Closing In: Curry and Gui Santos (20 pts) added two more buckets, trimming the deficit to one with 2:12 left
Then, Horford Hit Again: It was his fourth 3 in 3:35 of game time – and the Warriors’ first lead since 8:11 in the 2nd quarter, up two with 2:12 left
But It Was Short Lived: LA quickly answered at the line, tying the game with under two to play


The Warriors needed one more bucket.

So they went to Steph.

With all five defenders keyed on him, Curry gave it up to Draymond Green at the top — then got it right back on a dribble handoff. He looked to turn the corner, but Brook Lopez walled him off, so he stepped back and fired:

“Gooooooooood!”

Prime’s Kevin Harlan was in disbelief. The Intuit Dome was in disarray. Curry was in the front row, watching it drop.

Warriors up three with 50 seconds left.

Two steals from Green later, and Golden State was golden in the Play-In once again.

2. WARRIORS’ WAY: VETERAN CHAMPS LIFT GOLDEN STATE LATE

When the lights turn up, Steph Curry shines brightest.

Exactly one year from last night, on April 15, 2025, Curry scored 15 of his 37 points in the 4th quarter of a Play-In game to beat Memphis, sending Golden State to the Playoffs.

That’s what the four-time NBA champion does. Tenth in all-time Playoff points and first in Playoff 3s, Curry’s built for moments like these. Late, tight, season on the line — where time and time again, Curry delivers.

And he’s owned the SoFi Play-In stage.

Meeting The Moment: Steph has played in five career Play-In games. He’s scored 35+ points in four of them
Another Level: Only one other player has multiple career 35+ point performances in the Play-In: Ja Morant, with two
Always Dangerous: Curry also owns the highest scoring average in Play-In Tournament history (min. 3 GP), with a scorching 34 ppg
Unfazed Fire: That included playing past a minutes restriction last night as he works his way back from a knee injury, making multiple trips to the locker room before taking over
That Takeover? Seven 3s on 12-of-23 shooting, including a 10-of-14 mark in the 2nd half – capped by another clutch Curry banger
“All it takes is one,” said Steve Kerr postgame of Curry’s outbursts. “That’s his magic. He can will himself into a game. He has so much belief, so much competitive desire – it’s amazing to watch.”


That competitive desire fuels a veteran-laden Warriors squad with championship DNA – and it showed up Wednesday.

It started with Al Horford.

Worth The Wait: Horford, who won the 2024 NBA title with Boston, has battled through injuries in his first season with Golden State, missing 37 games
But he saved his biggest game for the biggest spot, scoring 12 in the 4th with his four late 3s – matching his highest-scoring quarter of the season
The night ended with Draymond Green.

Winning Hustle: After Curry’s go-ahead 3, Green stole LA’s inbound to set up a Brandin Podziemski bucket
10 Seconds Later: “Green stole the ball!” Another swipe, this time on Kawhi Leonard (21 pts, 7 ast), who Green limited to 2 points in the 4th
Winning Duo: Draymond finished with 7 points, 6 boards, 9 assists and a game-high 4 steals, as he and Curry reached the Playoffs together for the 11th time
“I’ve been watching them for 12 years,” said Kerr of Curry and Green. “There’s a reason we have four championships, and it’s the competitiveness. The heart. The will…
“It’s been such a tough year, but to just show what they’re made of – what we’re made of … with all the wins we’ve ever had here … this one is right up there.”
Up Next? Another elimination game, as the Warriors visit the Suns on Friday (10 ET) to decide the West’s No. 8 seed in the Playoffs. The loser goes home.

3. SIXERS CLAIM NO. 7 SEED: MAXEY MOVES PHILLY PAST ORLANDO

A year with its share of key injuries and absences.

An emergency appendectomy for franchise star Joel Embiid.

A Play-In elimination setting promising the unpredictable.

Questions continued to follow the 76ers and their 2025-26 aspirations through the finish of the regular season.

Wednesday night, Tyrese Maxey had an answer.

In front of Allen Iverson and the Philly faithful and in the classic black jerseys the team wore in the 2001 Finals, Maxey drove the Sixers back to the Playoffs after missing out last year.

Sixers 109, Magic 97: Maxey scored 11 of his 31 points in the 4th quarter, including a stretch of 7 straight that pulled Philly out of a one-possession game to a double-digit win, claiming the No. 7 seed for the Sixers and sending them to a First-Round series in Boston.

Desmond Bane’s game-high 34 points led the Magic, who now return home to host the 9-seed Hornets in the SoFi Play-In Tournament finale Friday to decide the 8-seed. | Recap

“I told [the team] at the beginning of the year, ‘I don’t care what it takes, we’re going to the Playoffs,’” Maxey said postgame
What It Took: Tyrese got his 31 efficiently but on a game-high 25 shots (44 FG%), leading all players with 42 minutes, following a season where he played the most minutes of his career (38 mpg)
Maxey’s Move: After a 24-14 Magic run made it 86-87 with under 8 minutes to go, Tyrese took off with a floater, a triple and another floater to stretch the lead back to 8
The Close: Orlando never got back within 4, with Maxey rising up for a game-sealing block in the final minute, before Andre Drummond (14 pts, 10 reb) delivered the dagger 3
“He’s been doing a bit of that lately… understanding we need his greatness at the right time,” coach Nick Nurse said of Maxey and his decisive 7-point burst.

Top-5 Ty: Maxey’s 31 is good for the 5th-highest scoring performance of this year’s Play-In Tournament, after ranking top-5 in scoring (career-best 28.3 ppg) throughout a season in which all his offensive averages increased
Winning Wings: Joining Maxey with over 40 minutes played Wednesday, VJ Edgecombe (11 reb) and Kelly Oubre Jr. (5 3s) contributed 19 points apiece
What’s Next: The 7-seed Sixers now face the 2-seed Celtics Sunday, the start of an NBA-record 23rd Playoff series between the two teams. No other postseason matchup has met more than 15 times


The Magic still have another chance to join the Sixers in that Playoff field, facing a Hornets squad that’s won its way into the Play-In finale, and six of its last eight games.

“There’s frustration, but it’s also a great opportunity ahead of us on Friday,” Bane said. “Get the chance to play at home in front of our fans… and the chance to go clinch a Playoff spot. I’m excited for the challenge.”
Orlando’s Outputs: After Bane’s 34 on Wednesday, the Magic got a combined 43 points from Paolo Banchero (18), Anthony Black (13) and Franz Wagner (12)
4. FRIDAY LOOK-AHEAD: WIN OR GO HOME ON PRIME

After two nights of 7 vs. 8 and 9 vs. 10 Play-In games, the stakes take another step up in Friday’s Play-In finale.

Win? A date with the No. 1 seed in the NBA Playoffs.

Lose? That’s a wrap on 2025-26.

We’ll dive more into each matchup in Friday’s edition, but let’s get you started here…

East | No. 10 Hornets at No. 8 Magic (7:30 ET, Prime): LaMelo Ball, Brandon Miller and Kon Knueppel lead Charlotte against Paolo Banchero, Desmond Bane and Franz Wagner in a battle of opposing styles.

3s Are Key: While Charlotte led the regular season in made 3s (16.4), no team allowed fewer triples than Orlando, which held its opponents to just 12.1 per game
Late Magic: Although the Magic couldn’t edge Philly last night, they’ve shined in crunch time this season with 21 clutch wins – tied with the Pistons for the most in the NBA
Buzzing Hornets: They’ll now face a Hornets squad coming off an electric OT win over Miami in Tuesday’s winner-take-all duel – moving them one win shy of their first Playoff berth in a decade
Up Next: The winner earns the East’s No. 8 seed, setting up a First Round matchup against the No. 1 Pistons (Game 1: Sunday at 6:30 ET, NBC)

West | No. 10 Warriors at No. 7 Suns (10 ET, Prime): Steph, Draymond & Co. look to complete their climb against Devin Booker, Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks.

Series Rewind: Golden State took three of four from Phoenix this season, with the Suns earning a last-minute 99-98 win Dec. 18 in which they held Curry to 2-of-9 from 3
Three & D Edge: Phoenix (45-37) finished 8 games ahead of Golden State and joined Boston as the only teams to rank top-5 in 3s (14.8 3pg) and top-10 in defense (112.9)
More History? The Dubs aim for a second road win to join last year’s Miami Heat as the only 10-seeds to advance out of the SoFi Play-In Tournament
OKC Awaits: The West’s No. 8 seed goes to the winner, sending them into the First Round against the defending-champion 1-seed Thunder (Game 1: Sunday at 3:30 ET, ABC)

5. AWAITING FRIDAY’S WINNERS: WHAT LIES AHEAD FOR THE NO. 1 SEEDS

Ask the last three NBA champions:

2025: No. 1-seed Thunder.

2024: No. 1-seed Celtics.

2023: No. 1-seed Nuggets.

The top seed in the Playoffs is a coveted spot to be in. But what are some of the challenges?

This year’s Thunder and Pistons, for instance, won’t have their First Round Playoff matchups solidified until Friday’s Play-In finale — making them the last seeds to have their opponent set.

NBA.com’s Shaun Powell examines the potential traps and hurdles OKC and Detroit could face on the Playoff road ahead, as they prepare for their Play-In challengers:

Being a top seed in the NBA playoffs has its privileges. Home-court advantage throughout the rounds and the most favorable matchups every round from a seeding standpoint are the obvious two…

[But] in the Playoffs, even with a seven-game series, sometimes the hotter team ultimately prevails, making all that hard work in the regular season moot. 

These are the realities for the top-seeded Detroit Pistons and Oklahoma City Thunder starting next weekend.” | Read More

Want to share Starting 5 with a friend? Send them this link.

Shape the Starting 5.  Email us here.

Don’t have the NBA App? Download it here.
